steps.n: These are individual letters that begin this chapter, written separately to show they are pronounced one at a time
Added the special marks (ٓ) that show these letters are pronounced separately and elongated
This is the basic feminine demonstrative pronoun meaning 'that'
Adding لْ shows we're pointing to something far away
Adding كَ completes the demonstrative pronoun. In this ayah, it's pointing to ءَايٰتُ (signs/verses) which comes right after it.

steps.m: to show, to be clear, to be apparent

steps.n: Forms a feminine noun meaning 'sign' or 'miracle' - something that shows or makes truth clear
Changes from one sign/miracle to many signs/miracles by replacing ة with ات - this is a common way to make feminine words plural in Arabic
Adds ُ to show this word is the subject being pointed to by تِلْكَ (those)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
Changes from a verb (wrote) to a noun (book/writing) by adding alif
The word gets 'the' (ال) at the start and changes ending to show it belongs to something
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: one who makes things clear (describes الكتاب - the Book)
steps.n: Changed to describe something that actively makes things clear. This word describes 'the Book' mentioned earlier in the ayah.

steps.m: the clear one (describes الكتاب - the Book)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and ِ to show it describes الكتاب (the Book)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed middle vowel to damma and dropped final vowel to make present tense stem
Added نَ at beginning to show 'we' are doing the action and added sukun on ت
Added silent alif at end to show multiple people doing the action
The ى at the end changes to ي when adding a suffix. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
Added ك at the end to show this is happening to 'you'. This word connects to نَتْلُوا۟ (We recite) to show what is being recited to you.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from. In this ayah, it connects 'We recite' (نَتْلُوا۟) with 'the news' (نَّبَإِ), showing what is being recited.
Connected the letters and added vowels to make it a noun meaning the news itself rather than the act of telling news
Added emphasis to show this is particularly important news
Added a kasra (small diagonal line) under the last letter to show this news belongs to Moses, like saying 'news of Moses'

steps.m: Moses (a name meaning 'drawn from water' or 'child of')
steps.n: This is a proper noun borrowed from Hebrew/Ancient Egyptian. It doesn't follow typical Arabic root patterns since it's a foreign name.

steps.m: Moses (a name meaning 'drawn from water' or 'child of')
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) on م and fatha (َ) on س to show correct pronunciation.

steps.m: Moses (a name meaning 'drawn from water' or 'child of')
steps.n: Added alif khanjariya (ٰ) which is a small alif written above the ى to indicate a long 'aa' sound at the end.
This is a borrowed word from ancient Egyptian, not derived from an Arabic root
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being talked about in the sentence
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to the previous mention of Moses
Connected the root letters and added fatha marks to show it's a past tense verb
Changed from a verb (action) to a noun (thing) by removing the final vowel
Added بِ at the beginning to mean 'with' or 'by means of'
Added ال (the) to make it specific 'the truth' and the kasra ending shows it follows the word بِ (with)
Changes verb root into a noun meaning 'a group that stands together'

steps.m: a people (being acted upon by لِ)
steps.n: Added tanween to show this is indefinite (any people) and is being affected by the following preposition
Added لِ to show this is 'for' or 'to' a people. This people are described in the next word as those who believe.
Added alif at beginning to show making someone else feel secure (through belief/trust)
Added يُ at start to show ongoing action in present/future. The آ changes to ؤ after يُ.
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action

Adding the shadda (ّ) changes this from a conditional word to an emphasis word. It makes the statement stronger, like adding an exclamation mark in English. Here it emphasizes the statement about Pharaoh that follows.

steps.m: Pharaoh (the title of ancient Egyptian kings)
steps.n: This is a proper noun borrowed from ancient Egyptian
Added a fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by إِنَّ (indeed/verily). This word is the object that إِنَّ is talking about in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb. This describes what Pharaoh did.
The و at the end changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end of a past tense verb
This is a basic preposition showing location. In this ayah, it shows where Pharaoh rose high/became arrogant - in the land.
The root letters are given vowels (fatha and sukun) to make the basic word for 'earth'

steps.m: the earth (in فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ - in the earth)
steps.n: Added ال (the) at beginning and kasra at end because it follows the word فِى (in)

steps.m: to make something into something else

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word is connected to يَسْتَضْعِفُ (he weakens) in the ayah, showing Pharaoh's actions.
Added و at beginning to connect this action with what came before it in the ayah, showing this was another thing Pharaoh did after becoming high and mighty in the land.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun

steps.m: her people (object of جَعَلَ - he made)
steps.n: Added ها at the end to show it belongs to her (referring to الأرض - the land). This word is what is being acted upon by جَعَلَ (he made).

steps.m: to split into groups or follow different paths

steps.n: Changed from basic root meaning 'split' into plural noun 'groups' by adding vowels i-a

steps.m: into (separate) groups (object of جَعَلَ - he made)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this word is receiving the action of 'making/dividing' in the verse. This connects to جَعَلَ (he made) earlier in the verse.
Added vowels to make basic past tense verb showing state of weakness
Added Form X pattern (است) at beginning to show considering or causing someone else to have this quality

steps.m: he considers/makes (someone) weak (subject of عَلَا - he exalted himself)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. This shows Pharaoh's ongoing action of oppressing people, mentioned after عَلَا (he exalted himself).

steps.m: to go around or move in a circle

steps.n: Root letters connected with vowels to make the basic noun form meaning 'going around'
Changed the middle و to ا and added ِ to show who does the action
Made the ا longer to show emphasis on the doer of the action

steps.m: a group (literally: those who go around together)
steps.n: Added ة to make it refer to a group

steps.m: a group (as the object of يَسْتَضْعِفُ - he oppresses)
steps.n: Added ً to show this group is being acted upon by the verb يَسْتَضْعِفُ (he oppresses)
This is a preposition that shows something coming from somewhere or belonging to something
The letter م has a shadda (double mark) to show it connects strongly to the next word

steps.m: from them (referring to the group of people Pharaoh oppressed)
steps.n: Added pronoun 'them' to show who is being referred to in the ayah

steps.m: to kill by cutting the throat

steps.n: Adding the shadda (ّ) on the middle letter shows this is being done repeatedly and violently to many victims

steps.m: he brutally slaughters many (referring to Pharaoh)
steps.n: The يُ at the start changes it from a command to 'he is' doing this brutal action repeatedly

steps.m: he brutally slaughters many (subject of يَسْتَضْعِفُ)
steps.n: The ُ at the end shows this word is connected to يَسْتَضْعِفُ (he oppresses) - both describing what Pharaoh does

steps.m: son (someone who continues the family building)
steps.n: Root meaning 'build' became 'son' - like how sons build and grow the family
Changed to plural form to show multiple sons

steps.m: their sons (the sons of the people Pharaoh was oppressing)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show these are the sons belonging to the oppressed people mentioned in the verse

steps.m: to be alive, to spare someone's life

steps.n: Adding vowels to make it a complete past tense verb meaning 'to live'
Added يَسْتَ at the beginning to mean 'to feel shy/embarrassed' - completely different meaning from original word

steps.m: and he keeps alive/spares life (connected to يُذَبِّحُ - he slaughters)
steps.n: Added و (and) to connect this action with the previous one in the verse. This shows contrast between Pharaoh slaughtering some and sparing others.
This is a special way to make the word mean 'multiple women'
The middle alif is made longer to show it's a large group

steps.m: women (as object of يُذَبِّحُ - he slaughters)
steps.n: Added a mark to show this word is receiving the action of slaughtering in the ayah

steps.m: their women (as object of يُذَبِّحُ - he slaughters)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show these are the women belonging to them (the Israelites)
This is a particle used to emphasize what comes after it. It makes the meaning stronger, like adding 'definitely' or 'truly' in English.
Added هُۥ at the end to show we're talking about 'he' (Pharaoh). This connects back to فِرْعَوْنَ mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Added fathas to make the basic past tense form before weak letter changes

The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels in a past tense verb. This is a common change in Arabic that makes the word easier to pronounce. In this ayah, it's telling us about a past state of Pharaoh.
This is a basic preposition that connects words to show where something comes from or what it belongs to
The َ (fatha) is added at the end because it comes before the word ال (the). This helps connect the words smoothly when speaking.
Added مُ at the start and ِ in the middle to show who does the action, like changing 'corrupt' to 'corrupter'
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple corrupters
Added ٱلْ at the start to point to specific corrupters, and َ at the end because it comes after مِنَ (from/of)
Connected the root letters with vowels to form basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he wanted' to 'we want' by adding نُ at start to make it 'we' and changing to present tense
Adds conjunction prefix
This particle connects 'we want' (نُرِيدُ) to what is wanted - showing what we want to do. It works like 'that' in English when you say 'I want that we go'
Added short 'a' sounds (fatha) and doubled the 'n' sound with a shadda mark to show 'he' did the action
Changed first vowel to damma to make it a command
Added ن at beginning to show 'we' as the doers
Added shadda on first ن to emphasize the action

This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or affecting something else. Here it connects the verb نَّمُنَّ (we bestow favor) to those receiving the favor (ٱلَّذِينَ ٱسْتُضْعِفُوا۟ - those who were oppressed).
The base word ذا (this/that) changes to ذِي to show it will connect to something else
Added ن at the end to show we're talking about many people
Added ال at the beginning with a shadda on the following letter to point to specific people - in this case, those who were oppressed
The vowel marks ضَعُفَ show this is about becoming weak (not just being weak)
Added است at the beginning to change from 'being weak' to 'making someone else weak' (like asking/causing someone to become weak)
Changed vowels to show this happened to someone rather than someone doing it

steps.m: they were made weak (referring to those who were oppressed)
steps.n: Added وا to show this happened to a group of people. This refers to those who were oppressed, mentioned in the previous part of the verse (ٱلَّذِينَ)
This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it connects 'were made weak' with 'the earth' to show where they were weakened.
Added short 'a' and sukoon marks to spell out 'earth/land'
Added ال to make it specific and ِ to show it follows فِى (in) in the ayah

steps.m: to make or set something as something else

steps.n: Added vowels to make it past tense 'he made' - like putting the pieces together to tell a story that already happened
Changed to present tense 'we' form by adding ن at start and adjusting vowels
Added pronoun suffix to show who is being made into something

steps.m: and we make them (connected to أَئِمَّةً - leaders)
steps.n: Added conjunction to connect this phrase with the word أَئِمَّةً (leaders) that comes before it in the verse

steps.m: to lead or be in front

steps.n: Changes from basic root to noun meaning 'leader'
Changed to broken plural form by adding ة at the end, adding أ at the start, and doubling the middle letter with shadda

steps.m: leaders (as the object being made in this verse)
steps.n: Added tanween (ً) to show this word is the object of the verb نَجْعَلَهُمْ (we make them)

steps.m: to make or set something as something else

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Changed to present tense 'we' form by adding ن at start and adjusting vowels
Added pronoun 'them' which refers to those who were oppressed (mentioned earlier in the ayah)
Added 'and' to connect this clause with previous statement, and changed final vowel due to connection with next word

steps.m: to inherit or receive something from someone else

steps.n: Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'inherited'
Changed from an action to someone who does the action

steps.m: inheritors (acted upon by نَجْعَلَهُمُ - we make them)
steps.n: Made the word plural to show multiple inheritors. This word is being acted upon by the verb نَجْعَلَهُمُ (we make them).

steps.m: the inheritors (acted upon by نَجْعَلَهُمُ - we make them)
steps.n: Added 'the' (ال) at the start to make it specific, and a fatha mark at the end because it's the object of 'we make them'

steps.m: to establish firmly or give power

steps.n: Doubled the middle letter to show doing something strongly or making someone else do it
Added نُ at start to show 'we' are doing the action, and changed vowels to match

steps.m: and we establish firmly (in relation to فِرْعَوْنَ)
steps.n: Added وَ to connect this with what comes before it. This word is part of what will happen to Pharaoh mentioned later in the verse.
Third person masculine plural pronoun
Adds the preposition ل meaning 'for/to'
This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it connects to الْأَرْضِ (the earth) to show location.
The ي changes to ى at the end of words. This is just a writing change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters and added basic vowel marks to make it pronounceable
Added ال to make it specific and ِ at the end because it comes after the word فِي (in/on)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense 'we' form by adding نُ at start
Added و conjunction prefix to connect with previous phrase about establishing them in the land
This is a proper noun that was the title for ancient Egyptian rulers. The vowel marks help with pronunciation.

steps.m: Pharaoh (as object of نُرِىَ - we show)
steps.n: The َ (fatha) at the end shows this word is receiving the action. Here, Allah is showing Pharaoh something.
This is a proper noun (name Haman) borrowed from another language. It refers to a minister of Pharaoh mentioned in the Quran.
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) above the letter. This is just a writing variation and doesn't change the meaning.
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence
Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and'

steps.m: to gather or assemble (like soldiers or armies)

steps.n: Root letters were given vowels to make a noun meaning 'army' from the basic idea of gathering
Changes from single army to multiple armies by adding و between letters
Added fatḥa to show this word is being acted upon
Added هُمَا to show the armies belong to two people (Pharaoh and Haman)
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Basic preposition meaning 'from' - added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation
Added هُ (meaning 'him/it') to mean 'from him/it' - not هُم (them) as stated in notes
This is a relative pronoun meaning 'what' or 'that which'. No morphological transformation needed as this is a primitive particle.

steps.m: that which (object of يَحْذَرُونَ - they were fearing)
steps.n: Added shadda for emphasis. This word connects to يَحْذَرُونَ (they were fearing) to show what they were afraid of.
Added fathas to make it past tense. This shows someone or something existed in the past.
Added alif after first letter because this is a hollow verb with و as middle root letter
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action. This word is the subject of يَحْذَرُونَ (they were fearing) in this ayah.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ونَ to show multiple people doing the action
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Added أ at beginning to show causing someone else to do the action. The ى at the end is because this is a weak verb.
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action
Added و at beginning to connect with previous statement, and lengthened the final alif
This is a preposition that shows direction or destination. Here it connects 'we revealed' to 'mother of Moses', showing who received the revelation.
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end which is a spelling variation of long 'aa' sound at the end of words
Added a small alif (ٓ) above the word which helps with pronunciation when continuing to the next word

steps.m: to be a mother, to lead as a source or origin

steps.n: The ُ (damma) sound makes it into the noun 'mother'
Added kasra at the end to show possession - this word is connected to Moses (Musa) in the ayah, showing it's his mother
This is a proper name borrowed from Hebrew/Egyptian. It doesn't come from an Arabic root. The name refers to Prophet Moses (peace be upon him).
Added an alif khanjariyah (ٰ) which is a small alif written above the ى to show it's pronounced as a long 'aa' sound
Added a small vertical line (ٓ) above the word to show a slight pause in recitation
This is a connecting word that introduces what was inspired/revealed. It connects the main verb أَوْحَيْنَآ (we inspired) to the next part about what should be done.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Added أ at start and sukun on first root letter to show causing someone else to do the action
Changed from 'he made nurse' to 'you make nurse!' by changing the vowels to make it a command
Added ي to show the command is for a woman
Added هِ to show who should be nursed (referring to baby Musa)
This is a time word that specifically points to future events, unlike other words for 'when'

steps.m: then/so when (connects this future event to what was mentioned before)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this to the previous statement about the inspiration given to Musa's mother. It shows this is what should happen next.

steps.m: to fear or be afraid of something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he feared' to 'you fear!' (command form) by changing vowels. The middle letter drops out.
Added تِ at the end to show this is talking about a female person in the past
The ى at the end changes to ي with sukun when something is attached after it

steps.m: upon him (connected to خِفْتِ - you feared)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show this means 'him'. This refers back to Moses (موسى) who was mentioned earlier in the verse.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Adding أ at start changes meaning to 'make someone do the action'
Added ف (meaning 'then/so') at start + changed ending to ي to make it a command for a female
Added هِ to show the object (Moses) being thrown
Basic preposition showing location. In this ayah, it connects to ٱلْيَمِّ (the sea) to show where to place baby Musa.

steps.m: to go towards, head in a direction

steps.n: Adding fatha makes it a past tense verb
The shadda makes the last م doubled for emphasis
The word 'sea' gets 'al' (the) in front and a kasra sound at the end because it comes after the word 'in'
This is a negative particle that tells someone not to do something. Here it's telling Musa's mother not to fear.

steps.m: and not (connects to previous command فَأَلْقِيهِ - throw him)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this command with the previous one. It's like saying 'throw him in the river AND do not fear'.
Connected the root letters with vowels. The middle weak letter و changes to ا when used in past tense.
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' and changed final vowel to ُ to show present tense
Changed from 'you fear' to 'do not fear' by adding ي at the end to make it a command for a female - used with لا
This is the basic negation word that stops something from happening
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this negation to what came before. Here it connects to the verb تَخَافِى (fear) to say 'and do not fear'
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form
Kasra ending is another way to show jussive mood
Added ي at the end to make it a negative command for a female (don't be sad). Works with لا
Word is already in final form with feminine command and negative meaning
This is a particle used at the start of sentences to add emphasis to what follows. It makes the next word take a special ending.
Added ا at the end which means 'we'. This connects to the next word رَآدُّوهُ (returning him) to say 'indeed we are returning him'.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and kasra after second letter to form active participle pattern
The two similar letters (د) combine with a shadda, and the alif is lengthened to آ
The damma at the end shows this word is in the nominative case
The و shows multiple people (they) and هُ means 'him'
This is a basic preposition showing direction or destination
Added كِ at the end to show this is addressing Moses's mother specifically. The ي with sukun (يْ) is added between the preposition and the pronoun to connect them smoothly.
This pattern shows someone/something doing the action
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to active participle pattern showing who does the action
Added plural ending to show multiple doers
Added pronoun suffix meaning 'him' (referring to Moses in this verse)

steps.m: and ones who make/place him (connected to رَآدُّوهُ)

This is a basic preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or belongs to something. Here it connects to 'the messengers' (ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ)
Added a fatha (َ) at the end because the next word starts with ال. This helps make the words flow together smoothly when reading.
Adding hamza at start makes the sending more purposeful or causative
Changes from a verb (sending) to describing the person/thing that receives the sending
Makes the word plural by adding ين, showing multiple people/things being sent
Makes the word definite by adding ال, like adding 'the' in English

steps.m: the ones who are sent (plural, accusative)
steps.n: Adds a fatha mark at the end to show the word is in the accusative case
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Adding اِ and ت shows extra effort - like going out of your way to pick something up
ف means 'and then' or 'so' - it shows this happened after something else

steps.m: and then he picked him up (referring to baby Musa)
steps.n: Added هُ (him) at the end to show baby Musa was the one being picked up

steps.m: and then he picked him up
steps.n: The elongation mark (ۥٓ) helps with proper Quranic recitation

steps.m: to be close to or connected with something

steps.n: Basic vowels (fatha and sukun) added to make the root letters readable as 'awl', keeping the meaning of closeness/connection
Changed to the special form meaning 'family/followers'. This word is connected to فِرْعَوْنَ (Pharaoh) in this ayah, showing whose family is being talked about.
This is the basic form of the proper noun for the ruler of ancient Egypt. It's a borrowed word that became part of Arabic.
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation: kasra (ِ) on ف, sukun (ْ) on ر, and sukun on و

steps.m: Pharaoh (as the object being spoken about)
steps.n: Added fatha (َ) at the end to show this word is the object being discussed in the sentence, mentioned alongside Haman (هٰمٰنَ)
Added basic vowels to connect the letters
ي at start shows present/future tense. The vowels change due to this being a hollow verb (with و in middle)

steps.m: so that he becomes (purpose for التقطه/they took him)
steps.n: لِ shows purpose - explains why Pharaoh's family took Moses. The final vowel changes to fatha because it follows لِ
Added fatha to show how the letter is pronounced
Added هُ (him) to show who it's for

steps.m: for/to them (referring to the family of Pharaoh)
steps.n: Changed هُ (him) to هُمْ (them) to show it's about multiple people

steps.m: to be hostile or cross boundaries

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to be hostile' into noun meaning 'enemy'
Added ًّا at the end to show this is what something becomes - like saying 'became an enemy'. The double و (with ّ) and ً with ا shows it's receiving the action.
Adding vowels to show this is a feeling/emotion verb in past tense
Changed from a verb (became sad) to a noun (sadness/grief) by adding an alif at the end, like the word 'enemy' later in the verse

steps.m: and a source of grief (object of لِيَكُونَ - to become)
steps.n: Added و (and) to connect this with عَدُوًّا (enemy). Both words are objects of لِيَكُونَ (to become) in the ayah
This is the basic form of the particle that shows condition or possibility

steps.m: indeed/truly (emphasizing what follows about فِرْعَوْنَ وَهٰمٰنَ وَجُنُودَهُمَا)
steps.n: Adding the shadda changes this from a conditional particle to an emphasis particle. In this ayah, it emphasizes the statement about Pharaoh, Haman, and their soldiers.
This is a proper noun borrowed from ancient Egyptian language meaning the royal palace, later used as the title for Egyptian kings

steps.m: Pharaoh (as the object being picked up by آلُ/the family of)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Pharaoh is the object being picked up in this sentence
This is a proper name of Pharaoh's chief minister mentioned in the Quran. Since it's a foreign name, it doesn't have an Arabic root
Added و (wa) at the beginning to connect this word with فِرْعَوْنَ (Pharaoh) that came before it, showing they are mentioned together
Added vowels to make the basic singular noun form
Changed the internal vowels to make it plural. This is a common pattern for making plurals in Arabic.
Added هُمَا which means 'their' when referring to exactly two people (in this case, Pharaoh and Haman)
The و at the beginning means 'and'
Added fatha marks to make the past tense form. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter in the middle.

steps.m: they were (referring to فِرْعَوْنَ وَهٰمٰنَ وَجُنُودَهُمَا - Pharaoh, Haman and their soldiers)
steps.n: Adding وا at the end makes it refer to a group of people (they) instead of just one person (he)

steps.m: to make a mistake or do wrong

steps.n: Added short vowels (fat-ha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he made a mistake'
Changed the word to describe someone who does the action
Added ين to show it's talking about many wrongdoers. This describes what they were (كَانُوا = they were).
Basic meaning of speaking or saying something
Added fathas and changed و to ا because it's a special kind of word. This makes it mean something that already happened.
Added ت with sukun at the end to show a female is doing the action
Added و with fatha at beginning to mean 'and'. Added kasra at end because this word connects to the next word قُرَّتُ (comfort) in the ayah.
Original meaning relates to being good or beneficial
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Changed from verb 'became a woman' to noun 'woman' by adding alif at start and changing vowels
Added feminine ت to specify this is a female/wife
Added damma to show this is the subject doing the action in قَالَتِ (she said)
This is a proper noun for the ruler of ancient Egypt
Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce the word correctly
The fatha at the end shows this word is being acted upon - in this case, it's what the wife is talking about
Basic meaning of coolness or settling
Added emphasis to show strong feeling of coolness/comfort
Added feminine marker ت to match the subject

steps.m: to see or relate to eyes
steps.n: Basic meaning related to seeing
Basic noun form meaning a physical eye
Added tanween kasra at the end to show this is the object being described as bringing comfort/coolness in the phrase قُرَّتُ عَيْنٍ (comfort of the eye)
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or purpose
The shadda shows emphasis and indicates that another ل merged into this one

steps.m: for me, to me (in قُرَّتُ عَيْنٍ لِّى - a comfort of the eye for me)
steps.n: Adding ى at the end shows this is happening to or for 'me'
Added كَ to show this is 'for you' specifically. This كَ means it's directed to one person being spoken to.

steps.m: and for you (connected to قُرَّتُ عَيْنٍ - comfort of [the] eye)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to the previous phrase 'comfort of the eye'. The و here joins two benefits of baby Musa - he's a comfort to Pharaoh's wife AND for Pharaoh himself.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَشْعُرُونَ (they perceive) into 'they do not perceive'. It's like adding 'not' before a verb in English.
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Adding ت at the start makes it a present tense verb for 'you'
Adding و suffix changes the subject from singular to plural masculine 'you'

steps.m: you all kill him (referring to baby Musa)
steps.n: Added هُ to show the object (him) is baby Musa, as mentioned in the context of this ayah where Pharaoh's wife is speaking about not killing him
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a past tense verb

steps.m: perhaps, maybe (introducing what أَن يَنفَعَنَآ follows)
steps.n: The word changes to mean 'perhaps' when followed by أَن (that). Here it introduces the hope that 'he may benefit us'
This is a connecting particle that links عَسَىٰٓ (perhaps) to what follows. It helps express the hope that something might happen.
Basic meaning of being beneficial
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added يَ at start to show present tense, with sukun after first root letter
Added نَا at the end to show who receives the benefit (us)
The آ shows we hold the 'aa' sound longer at the end

steps.m: or (connecting قُرَّتُ عَيْنٍ with نَتَّخِذَهُۥ وَلَدًا)
steps.n: This is a basic conjunction word that gives a choice between two options. In this ayah, it connects two possible benefits of the child: being a comfort to their eyes or becoming their son.
Basic meaning of taking something
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Form VIII verb showing someone doing something for themselves
Changed to present tense 'we' form and added kasra because it comes after أن
Added pronoun 'him' to show who is being taken. In this ayah, Pharaoh's wife is suggesting they take baby Musa as their son.
Basic meaning related to birth and children
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic noun form

steps.m: a child (object of نَتَّخِذَ - we take)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this is the object being taken/adopted in the phrase 'or we take him as a child'
Added vowel marks to show proper pronunciation
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This word is the subject (doer) of يَشْعُرُونَ (they perceive) in this ayah.
This is a negative particle that makes the command تَقْتُلُوهُ (kill him) into a prohibition 'do not kill him'. In this ayah, it's part of Pharaoh's wife telling others not to kill baby Musa.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they sense (referring to the disbelievers in the story)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters and make it a past tense verb
Adding أَ changes it from just describing morning to describing entering any state. This verb connects to فٰرِغًا (empty) to show what state was entered.
Adding conjunction prefix to connect to previous phrase
Changes from basic root to noun meaning heart/core
Added ُ at the end to show this is the subject (doer) of the verb أَصْبَحَ (became/became empty) in this ayah

steps.m: to be a source or origin

steps.n: Added damma (ُ) at start and doubled the middle letter to make the word for 'mother'
Added kasra at the end to show possession - this word is connected to Moses (مُوسَىٰ) in the ayah, showing it's his mother
This is a proper name borrowed from Hebrew. It doesn't follow Arabic root patterns.

Added diacritical marks to show proper pronunciation. The ىٰ with alif above it is a special way of writing the long 'aa' sound at the end of foreign names. This word is the subject of كَادَتْ (she almost) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: being empty (describes the heart of Moses's mother)
steps.n: Changes the verb into a descriptive word showing the state of being empty
The word gets a special mark over the alif (ٰ) and tanween ending (-an) to show it's describing how empty the heart became
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. Here it connects with كَادَتْ (she was about to) to show what would have happened if Allah hadn't strengthened her heart.
Added kasra mark under the إ to show correct pronunciation
The root letters got vowels, and the middle letter و changed to ا to make it easier to say - this happens a lot with words that have و in the middle

steps.m: she nearly did (subject of تُبْدِى - she would reveal)
steps.n: Added ت with sukun at the end to show the subject is feminine (referring to the mother of Moses)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added ت at start to make it 'she' and changed to present tense ('reveals' instead of 'revealed')
Added لَ at beginning to show emphasis and certainty. In this context, it shows something that almost happened.
Basic preposition showing relationship or connection
Added pronoun suffix to show who the preposition connects to. Here it refers back to Moses (موسى) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Added small alif (ۦ) after the pronoun as a pronunciation helper
This is the basic conditional word that introduces a hypothetical scenario
Adding لَا (not) changes the meaning to express something that would have happened if not for something else

steps.m: if not for (connecting to أَن رَّبَطْنَا)
steps.n: The alif is lengthened to show connection with what follows. Here it connects to 'that We strengthened' (أَن رَّبَطْنَا)
This is a foundational particle that connects clauses, showing what might happen. Here it connects to لَتُبْدِى (she would reveal) to show what almost happened.
Added fatha mark (َ) above أ to show correct pronunciation.
Adding fatha vowels creates the basic past tense verb form
Adding نا changes the subject to 'we' and shows we did the action in the past
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of or over something else. It's one of the core words in Arabic that doesn't come from a root.
Added the small alif (ٰ) after the ى to show it's a long vowel sound. In this ayah, it shows something being placed over her heart specifically.
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to create a noun meaning the physical heart, which is named this way because it constantly turns blood through the body
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to something after it (showing possession)
Added ها at the end to show the heart belongs to her (Moses's mother). This heart is being acted upon by the word رَّبَطْنَا (We strengthened/made firm)
Connected the root letters with damma to form the basic verb pattern
Added تَ at the start to show 'you' or 'she/it' is doing the action
Changed the final vowel to fatha to show this is dependent on another verb
Added لِ to show purpose - this explains why something else happens
Basic preposition showing something comes from somewhere or belongs to something
Added fatha at the end to connect it with the next word (ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ). This shows it's part of the phrase 'from the believers'
Added مُ at the start to show 'the one who' does the action, with ؤ between first and second root letters to mean 'believes/trusts'
Added ين to show multiple believers. This word follows مِنَ (from) in the ayah, which requires this ending.
Added ٱلْ to specify these particular believers, and َ at the end because it follows مِنَ
Connected the letters and changed و to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter). Added fathas to show it's past tense.
Added ت with sukun at the end to show a female is doing the action
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this with what came before. In this ayah, she is speaking to her sister.
Changes the word into feminine form by adding damma, sukun and taa
The kasra (ِ) at the end shows this word is the object of a preposition
The هِ at the end means 'his', showing who the sister belongs to
Added لِ meaning 'to' at the beginning and ۦ at the end which is a pronunciation mark for هِ
Adds لِ ('to/for') in front of 'his sister' to show direction/purpose

steps.m: to follow or track something by following its traces

steps.n: Added vowels and doubled middle letter (shadda) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he followed'
Changed the vowels to show something was done to it rather than it doing the action
Added ي to make it a command for a female (Moses' sister)

steps.m: follow him closely (command to Moses' sister)
steps.n: Added هِ to show who should be followed (baby Moses). This is what Moses' mother commanded his sister to do.

steps.m: to see or watch from a distance

steps.n: Added vowel marks (fatha and damma) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to watch carefully'
ت shows it's feminine
Added sukun at end to show end of word. The فَ shows this happened as a result of what came before - after his sister was told to follow him.
This is a preposition that shows how something is done

steps.m: with/by him (referring to the baby Moses)
steps.n: Added هِ to show this refers to him (the baby Moses in this story)

steps.m: with/by him (referring to the baby Moses)
steps.n: Added small ۦ after هِ to help with pronunciation, but doesn't change the meaning
Basic preposition showing distance or separation. In this ayah, it connects to جُنُبٍ (from a distance) to show how someone was watching from afar.

steps.m: from a distance (describes how فَبَصُرَتْ/she watched was done)
steps.n: Root ج-ن-ب became جُنُب (side/distance) then جُنُبٍ (from a distance) to show how she watched - like saying 'from the side'
Basic pronoun for 'they' (masculine). This pronoun is the subject of the verb يَشْعُرُونَ (they perceive/realize) in this ayah.
Added conjunction وَ (and) to connect this clause with the previous one
Basic negative particle that negates the verb يَشْعُرُونَ (they perceive/realize). In this ayah, it shows that 'they do not perceive/realize'.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense with يَ prefix and vowel changes
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This word comes after لَا (not) in the ayah, so it means 'they do not sense'
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning more intense or emphatic

steps.m: we strictly forbade (acted on المراضع/the wet-nurses)
steps.n: Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action, and added sukun before it. This verb acts on المراضع (the wet-nurses) in the ayah.
Added و at beginning to connect this action with what came before
This is a basic preposition showing something is on top of or over something else
Changed ى to يْ to prepare for adding the pronoun. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect meaning.

steps.m: upon him (referring to the nursing child in the story)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show this is happening to 'him'. In this ayah, it refers to baby Musa who they were trying to find a wet nurse for.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Added مَ at start to show place/person, added ا after ر to make it 'one who does', then made plural by changing vowels
Adds definite article to make the noun definite
Shows this word is being acted upon by the verb وَحَرَّمْنَا (we forbade)
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts from or comes from. Here it combines with قَبْلُ (before) to mean "from before"
Added the kasra (ِ) mark under the م to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'min'

steps.m: to be in front of or before something

steps.n: The root letters got vowel marks (fatha and sukun) to make the word mean 'before' instead of the action of being in front
Added damma (ُ) at the end to show this word is in the nominative case

steps.m: to speak, tell, or say something

steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter). This is a common pattern in Arabic.
Adding تْ at the end changes 'he' to 'she' in the past tense. The sukun (ْ) shows the end of the word.

steps.m: and then she said (introducing her suggestion about هَلْ أَدُلُّكُمْ)
steps.n: Adding فَ shows this happened after something else. Here, it introduces what she says next about finding someone to take care of the baby.
This is a question word that turns a statement into a yes/no question. In this ayah, it's used to ask 'Shall I guide you?' (هَلْ أَدُلُّكُمْ)
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation: fatha (َ) on ه and sukun (ْ) on ل

steps.m: to guide or show the way

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Combined the repeated letters with shadda to show emphasis
Added hamza prefix and changed vowels to make it first person present tense

steps.m: I guide you all (referring to the people being addressed in this ayah)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show who is being guided - in this case, the people being spoken to
Basic preposition showing something is on top of or upon something else
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation: fatha on ع and ل
Added alif maksura with superscript alif (ىٰ) at the end to show proper writing style
Added small alif (ٓ) above the word to show proper Quranic writing style
Root letters got vowel marks (fatha-sukun) to make the common noun meaning 'family'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by بَيْتٍ (house), forming the phrase 'family/household of a house'

steps.m: to house, shelter, or provide overnight accommodation

steps.n: Root letters ب-ي-ت become the simple noun 'house' by adding vowel marks. Like saying 'home' instead of 'to live'. This word connects to أَهْلِ (family) to mean 'people of the house'.
Added the tanween kasra (two small diagonal lines under) at the end to show this is an indefinite noun that belongs to أَهْلِ (family/people of).

steps.m: to care for or be responsible for

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action

steps.m: they care for him (subject of أَهْلِ بَيْتٍ)
steps.n: Added هُ to show who is being cared for. This word is part of أَهْلِ بَيْتٍ (people of a household) who will care for baby Musa.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation

steps.m: for you all (being addressed by أَدُلُّ)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this is directed at multiple people. This word is connected to the verb أَدُلُّ (I guide) in this ayah, showing who is being guided.
This is a pronoun meaning 'they' that refers to multiple people. It's the subject doing the action in نٰصِحُونَ (being sincere/advisors).
Adding وَ connects this word to what came before it in the sentence, joining multiple ideas together
This is a basic particle that shows direction or ownership
Adding the fatha mark helps with pronunciation

steps.m: for him (referring to يَكْفُلُونَهُۥ - they would take care of him)
steps.n: Adding هُ shows this is 'for him', referring back to the baby Moses who they would take care of
The ۥ mark is just a writing style that shows the هُ is pronounced with a long vowel

steps.m: to give sincere advice or guidance

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed the pattern to show someone who does the action of advising
Added damma to prepare for plural ending
Added ون to make it plural. This word describes هم (they) in the sentence, showing that they are sincere advisors.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this is a past tense action. The double د at the end shows the action is done with force or emphasis.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this action happened as a result of or following something else mentioned before
Changed from 'he' to 'we' by adding نٰ at the end. The ْ (sukun) is added on the last د because you can't have two vowels in a row when adding a suffix.

steps.m: so we returned him (to أُمِّهِ his mother)
steps.n: Added هُ to show who was returned (him/it). This connects to the next words إِلَىٰٓ أُمِّهِۦ which tell us he was returned 'to his mother'.
This is a preposition that shows direction or movement towards something. In this ayah, it connects 'We returned him' (فَرَدَدْنٰهُ) with 'his mother' (أُمِّهِ) to show where he was returned to.
Added an alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end which is just a writing variation of ى. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added a small alif (ٓ) above the word. This is just a pronunciation mark that shows you should stretch the sound a little.
Added damma and shadda to create the basic noun form for 'mother'
Added kasra to show this word follows the preposition إِلَىٰٓ (to)
Added هِۦ to show the mother belongs to him

This is a particle that shows purpose or intention. In this ayah, it connects to the verb تَقَرَّ (become cool/content) to explain why Allah returned baby Musa to his mother - so that her eye would become content.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Doubles the middle root letter ر with shadda to show intensity
Adding تَ shows the action happens naturally. This verb describes how her eye (عَيْنُهَا) becomes content when seeing her child.
Connected the root letters with fatha and sukun to make the basic noun 'eye'. This word is the subject of تَقَرَّ (become cool/content) in this ayah.
Added damma to show this is the subject doing the action in تَقَرَّ (become cool/content)

steps.m: her eye (as the doer of تَقَرَّ)
steps.n: Added هَا to show it belongs to her (Musa's mother). The eye is doing the action of becoming cool/content in تَقَرَّ.
This is a basic negation word that says 'not' or 'no' to something
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this with what came before. In this ayah, it's connecting two purposes: that her eye be cooled (satisfied) AND that she not grieve.
Added vowels to show this is about feeling sad in the past
Added تَ (ta) at start and changed vowels to make it 'she becomes sad' in present tense, since it's about Moses's mother
Added fatha (َ) at the end to show this is part of a larger phrase meaning 'so that she would not be sad'
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make past tense 'he knew'
Added ت at beginning to make it 'she' and changed the pattern to show ongoing action (present tense)

steps.m: so that she knows (connected to تَقَرَّ and تَحْزَنَ)
steps.n: Added لِ to show purpose - connects with 'so her eye becomes cooled and she does not grieve'

steps.m: and so that she knows (connected to تَقَرَّ and تَحْزَنَ)
steps.n: Added وَ to connect this purpose with the previous purposes mentioned (her eye being cooled and not grieving)
This is the basic form of the particle that connects and introduces new information

steps.m: that, indeed (emphasizing what follows وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ)
steps.n: Adding the shadda (doubling mark) on ن makes the meaning stronger, emphasizing that Allah's promise is definitely true
Changed from the root meaning 'to promise' into the noun 'a promise' by adding vowel marks

steps.m: promise (object of لِتَعْلَمَ - so that she would know)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the verb لِتَعْلَمَ (so that she would know). The promise being referred to here is Allah's promise, which is clarified by the word اللَّهِ (of Allah) that comes right after it in the ayah.
Added shadda to emphasize this is the one and only God
Added kasra at the end to show this is the object of the word وَعْدَ (promise) in this ayah. Also changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word.
Connected the root letters and doubled the middle letter to show this is a strong, definite truth. This word describes Allah's promise (وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ) in this ayah.
Added double damma (tanween) at the end to make it indefinite, meaning 'a truth' instead of 'the truth'

steps.m: but (emphasizing the contrast with what came before)
steps.n: This is an emphatic particle that shows strong contrast. The ّ (shadda) at the end makes the meaning stronger.

steps.m: and yet/however (connecting to previous statement about Allah's promise being true)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this contrasting statement with what came before. This shows that even though Allah's promise is true, most people don't know it.
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added أَ at beginning and changed vowels to make comparative form meaning 'more/most numerous'

steps.m: most of them (referring to the people who don't know Allah's promise is true)
steps.n: Added هُمْ with fatha before it to show possession/relation to 'them', referring to the people mentioned in the context who don't understand that Allah's promise is true
This is a negative particle that negates the verb تَحْزَنَ (she grieves), making it mean 'she does not grieve'
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Added يَ at the beginning and changed vowels to show present tense
Added ون at the end to show multiple people doing the action
This is a particle that shows one event happened right after another, like saying 'as soon as' or 'when' in English. In this ayah, it connects to بَلَغَ (he reached) to show what happened next.

steps.m: and when (indicates a sequence of events)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English.

steps.m: to reach or arrive at something
he reached (subject of أَشُدَّهُۥ - his strength/maturity)
steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word connects to أَشُدَّهُۥ which means 'his strength/maturity', so together they mean 'he reached his maturity'.
Connected the root letters and added shadda to show emphasis
Added أَ at start and damma at end to make it mean 'more/most'
Added هُ to show it's his strength. This whole phrase means 'he reached his full strength/maturity'

steps.m: to be level, complete, or mature

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to show something happening gradually until completion
Added و at beginning to connect with previous phrase about reaching full strength
Added alif maksura and elongation mark for pronunciation

steps.m: to give, bring, or come with something

steps.n: Changed first letter to hamza with fatha and added long alif to make Form IV verb (makes action affect others). The final ي changes to ى because it's at the end.
The final ى changes back to يْ (with sukun) because more letters will be added after it
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action

steps.m: we gave him (something mentioned in ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ)
steps.n: Added هُ to show who received the action. The alif in نَا changes to ٰ when connecting with هُ
Changed the action of judging into the wisdom or judgment that comes from it
Added -an ending to show this specific wisdom was given as a gift, linking to 'We gave him' in the verse
Changed the root into a noun that means the concept of knowledge itself
Added tanween to show this is what was given to him. This word is connected to ءَاتَيْنٰهُ (We gave him) in the ayah.
The و connects it to the previous word
This is the basic demonstrative word meaning 'this'. The alif is written with a special mark (dagger alif) in this case.
Adding لِكَ changes 'this' to 'that', making it point to something farther away
Adding كَ at the beginning makes it mean 'like that' or 'similarly'

steps.m: and like that (connects to ءَاتَيْنٰهُ حُكْمًا وَعِلْمًا - We gave him wisdom and knowledge)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this to the previous statement, showing that just as We gave him wisdom and knowledge, similarly We reward those who do good
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb

steps.m: we reward (those who do good, referring to المُحْسِنِينَ)
steps.n: Added نَ at beginning to show 'we' are doing the action, and changed vowels to show it's happening now or in the future. The ى at the end shows it's ongoing.

steps.m: to do good, to be beautiful

steps.n: Added the vowels fat-ha (َ) and damma (ُ) to make it mean 'he was good/beautiful' in the past tense
Added مُ at start to show the doer, changed pattern to show someone who regularly does good deeds
Added ين to make it plural (more than two)
Added ٱلْ to make it specific and َ at the end to show it's being acted upon in the sentence

steps.m: to enter, go in, or come inside

steps.n: Added vowels to show this is a complete past tense verb. This word is connected to فَوَجَدَ (then he found) in the ayah, showing a sequence of actions.
Added وَ at the beginning to mean 'and'

steps.m: to settle or build a permanent place

steps.n: Changes the word to mean 'a place where people settle'
Makes the word feminine and specific to mean 'city'
Added ال to specify this particular city
Added fatha at the end to show this is what was entered (the object of the verb دَخَلَ/entered)
This is the basic form showing something being in a higher position
Adding ى at the end makes it work as a preposition that connects to what comes after it
The small alif (ٰ) shows this is the end of the word and helps with pronunciation. Here it connects to 'time' to show when something happened.
Adding the kasra vowel makes this a noun meaning 'time'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to غَفْلَةٍ (unawareness) that follows it, forming the phrase 'time of unawareness'
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to create the base noun
Adds ة to show one specific instance or period of being unaware/heedless

steps.m: a period of unawareness (in حِينِ غَفْلَةٍ - a time of unawareness)
steps.n: The word gets a kasra (small diagonal line) at the end because it comes after حِينِ (time of), showing they are connected to mean 'time of unawareness'
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it belongs to
The م gets doubled (with ّ) because it connects smoothly to the word before it when speaking. The ْ shows where the word stops.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to form the basic noun meaning 'people/family'
Added kasra at the end to show possession (this word belongs to something else)
Added ها to show this refers to the city (which is feminine in Arabic)
Adding fatha vowels creates the basic past tense verb meaning 'he found'
Adding فَ at the beginning shows this action happened after something else in the story
Basic preposition meaning 'in'. This word is a particle, so it doesn't come from a root.
Added ها to show it refers to المدينة (the city), which is feminine. This word connects to وَجَدَ (he found) in the ayah, showing where he found the two men fighting.

steps.m: to be a man, to walk like a man

steps.n: The vowels make it into a noun meaning 'man'
Added َيْنِ at the end to show exactly two men. These are the two men who were fighting, as shown by يَقْتَتِلَانِ (they two were fighting) in the next part of the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding ت shows the action is being done between multiple people - they are fighting each other
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. Shows two people fighting each other
Added انِ to show exactly two people are involved in the mutual fighting
Basic demonstrative element meaning 'here' or 'this'
Added ذا to specify this is for masculine singular objects that are nearby
Added alif with maddah (ٰ) and fatha marks to show proper pronunciation. This word is pointing to عَمَلِ (deed/action) in this ayah.

steps.m: from (showing this person belongs to شِيعَتِهِۦ - his followers)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition showing where something comes from or what group someone belongs to. In this ayah, it's used to show which group each of the two fighting men belonged to.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show passive form
The ة makes this a noun meaning 'a group of followers'
Added 'i' sound at the end to show this group belongs to someone
Added هِۦ to show these are Moses's followers
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun meaning 'this' for something nearby that is masculine. The long alif (ا) at the end shows it's masculine.
Added و with fatha (وَ) at the beginning to mean 'and'. This connects this phrase to what came before it.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it shows that the enemy's side is where one of the men came from.

steps.m: to be hostile, to act as an enemy

steps.n: Added shadda (ّ) on و to show intensity of the characteristic. This makes it mean someone who is very hostile or consistently acts as an enemy.
Adds genitive case marker to indicate possession
Adds هِ to show possession ('his') plus ۦ which is a small mark showing the word connects to what comes after it. This word comes after مِنْ (from/of) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb. The و changes to ا because it's between two vowels.
The است prefix shows someone is seeking or requesting something

steps.m: he sought his help (referring to the man from his people)
steps.n: Added هُ (hu) at the end to show who he sought help from - the man from his own people mentioned earlier in the verse

steps.m: so he sought his help (after finding the two men fighting)
steps.n: Added فَ (fa) at the beginning to show this happened right after he found the two men fighting
This is the base form of the relative pronoun used to connect parts of a sentence, like 'who' or 'which' in English

steps.m: the one who (referring to the enemy mentioned in عَدُوِّهِۦ)
steps.n: Adding ٱلّ makes it more specific, like saying 'the one who' instead of just 'who'. In this ayah, it refers back to the person from Moses's enemy's side.

steps.m: from (showing this person belongs to شِيعَتِهِۦ - his followers)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows belonging or origin. In this ayah, it shows that one of the men fighting belongs to Moses's followers.
Changed from a root meaning 'follow' into a noun meaning 'group of followers' by adding vowels
Added ة to make it refer to one specific group
Added ِ (kasra) at the end to connect this word to what comes after it, showing it belongs to something
Added هِۦ at the end to show these are his followers. This word comes after مِن (from/of) in the ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of, over, or during something else. Here it connects to حِينِ to show something happened 'during/at a time'
This is a relative pronoun used to connect descriptions to nouns. The ل of ال assimilates with ذ causing shadda.

steps.m: from (showing عَدُوِّهِۦ belongs to the enemy group)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows something belongs to or comes from a group or place. Here it shows that one of the men belongs to Moses's enemy group.
Added vowels to create the basic noun pattern for someone who does the action
Doubling of the final weak letter و to form the noun 'enemy'
The word changes slightly to show it's connected to 'his' and comes after a word meaning 'from/of'
The هِ (hi) is added at the end to mean 'his', changing 'enemy of' to 'his enemy'
The ۦ is added as a pronunciation helper after هِ, it doesn't change the meaning
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this is something that happened in the past. Moses (موسى) is the one who did the striking.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this action happened as a result of what came before it in the story

steps.m: so he struck him (done by موسى)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show who received the action (the person who was struck)

This is a proper noun, the name of the prophet Moses. In this ayah, he is the subject who struck someone. The name may come from Egyptian origins meaning 'born of' or 'child', though some say it comes from مَسَى meaning 'to walk' or 'to draw out' (as he was drawn from water).
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make past tense. This word describes what Musa did before he spoke (قَالَ).
The final ي with fatha (يَ) changes to ى because it's at the end of the word

steps.m: so he ended it (referring to the fight)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happened as a result of what came before. The small alif (ٰ) helps with pronunciation.
This is a preposition (عَلَى) combined with the pronoun (هِ) meaning 'upon him'
These are the three root letters that give us the basic meaning of speaking or saying
Added fathas to make it past tense, showing someone did this action in the past
Changed the weak letter و to ا which is very common in hollow verbs
Basic attention-drawing word that helps point to things
Added ذا to make it point to something specific. The long alif (ٰ) shows it's pointing to something nearby. In this ayah, it's pointing to one of two men who are fighting.

steps.m: from (showing that عَدُوِّهِۦ belongs to/comes from his enemies)
steps.n: Basic preposition showing where something comes from, starts, or belongs to. In this ayah, it shows that one of the men belongs to/comes from Moses's enemies.
Adding the basic vowel pattern to make it a past tense verb
Added kasra at the end to show this deed belongs to Satan (الشَّيْطٰنِ) in the ayah

steps.m: to be rebellious or act like an enemy

steps.m: indeed/verily (emphasizes that he is عَدُوٌّ مُّضِلٌّ مُّبِينٌ)
steps.n: Emphasis particle that makes what comes after it stronger and more certain
Adds 'he/it' pronoun suffix to show we're talking about Satan mentioned earlier
Added fatha on first letter and damma on second letter to create pattern for someone who does the action
The word gets a double 'w' sound at the end to show it means 'a strong enemy' or 'someone very hostile'

steps.m: one who makes others go astray
steps.n: Changes root into 'doer' form - someone who leads others away from right path
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger - someone who frequently/intensely leads others astray
Adding ٌ makes the word indefinite (a misleader vs the misleader)

steps.m: to make clear or show plainly

steps.n: Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to show active making something clear
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show someone/something that does the action
Added shadda to emphasize the clarity
Added tanween to show this describes the enemy (عَدُوٌّ) mentioned in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb. This word introduces what he says to his Lord (رَبِّ) in this ayah.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter). This is a common change that makes it easier to pronounce.

steps.m: Lord (one who nurtures and cares for all)
steps.n: Doubling the middle letter makes it refer to the one who does this action perfectly
Adding kasra at the end makes it into a call or plea to the Lord
This is a special word that adds emphasis to what comes after it. It's like adding 'definitely' or 'truly' at the start of a sentence.

steps.m: indeed I (speaking to رَبِّ - my Lord)
steps.n: Added ى at the end to show 'I' am speaking. The نّ gets a kasra (ِ) sound before ى according to Arabic rules. This word connects to رَبِّ (my Lord) as the person being spoken to.
Added fathas to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb

steps.m: I wronged (subject saying this to رَبِّ)
steps.n: Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did the action. This is part of what someone is saying to their Lord (رَبِّ) in this ayah.
Root letters n-f-s turned into a simple noun meaning 'soul/self'. In this sentence, it's the thing being wronged.
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb ظَلَمْتُ (I wronged).

steps.m: my soul, my self (acted on by ظَلَمْتُ)
steps.n: Added ى at the end to show this is my soul/self.

steps.m: to cover up faults/sins, to forgive

steps.n: Added فَ meaning 'so/then' to show this follows from saying 'I wronged myself'
Basic preposition
Added ى to show this means 'me'. This word connects to فَٱغْفِرْ (forgive) to show who is being forgiven - Moses is asking Allah to forgive him.

steps.m: he forgave (in response to فَٱغْفِرْ لِى - forgive me)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense. This word answers Musa's request for forgiveness (فَٱغْفِرْ لِى - forgive me).

steps.m: so He forgave (Allah's response to فَٱغْفِرْ لِى)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this was Allah's response to Musa's request for forgiveness. The فَ shows the forgiveness happened because of the request.

Added هُ which means 'him', showing who the forgiveness was for
Added pronunciation helpers: ۥ shows the 'u' sound is extended, and ٓ shows a slight pause is possible here

steps.m: indeed he (referring to Allah being forgiving)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show 'he/him'. This connects to the next ayah which describes Allah as الْغَفُورُ (The Most Forgiving).

steps.m: indeed he (referring to Allah being forgiving)
steps.n: Added the small و mark (ۥ) after هُ. This is just a writing style that doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: he (referring to Allah in this context)
steps.n: Independent pronoun for 'he'. In this ayah, it refers back to Allah who is described as being الْغَفُورُ الرَّحِيمُ (the Most Forgiving, the Most Merciful).

steps.m: The Most Forgiving (one of Allah's names)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite and ُ at the end to show it's the subject. When used as Allah's name, it means He is the ultimate source of forgiveness.

steps.m: The Intensely Merciful (one of Allah's names)

Added fathas to make it past tense. This word is connected to رَبِّ (my Lord) which follows it, showing who Musa is speaking to.
The و changes to ا because it's between two fathas, which is a common rule for weak letters
The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes this a title/role, emphasizing complete nurturing authority
Adding kasra at the end makes this a call/plea to one's Lord
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation
Added بِ at the beginning to show cause/reason. This connects to the next word أَنْعَمْتَ (You bestowed favor), explaining why something happened.
Changed the final alif to a long alif (آ) to show proper pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Adding أ at the start changes the meaning from 'to be blessed' to 'to make someone blessed'
Adding تَ shows that 'you' (a male) did this action in the past
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation of the preposition
Changed ى to ي with sukun to prepare for adding the pronoun
Added shadda and fatha to show strong attachment of pronoun ي meaning 'me'

Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters

steps.m: I am/will be (in فَلَنْ context: I will not)
steps.n: Added أَ at beginning to show first person singular. The fatha ending is because this verb comes after فَلَنْ (so/then will not) in the ayah, which puts the verb in the subjunctive mood.

steps.m: to help, support, or back up

Changed the pattern to create a noun meaning 'one who helps/supports'
Added tanween fatha to show this is the object of the verb أَكُونَ (I will be)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb

Added لِ to show direction/purpose and ال to make it specific

Basic preposition showing location or containment. Here it connects with the word ٱلْمَدِينَةِ to show where the action takes place.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Changes the word to mean 'a place where people settle'
Makes the word feminine and specific to mean 'city'
Added kasra to show this city is connected to something else mentioned in the ayah
Added ال to show this refers to a specific city that was known

This is a basic particle that shows something happens unexpectedly or at a specific time
Added فَ at the beginning to connect this with what happened before, showing one thing follows another
This is the basic form of the word that connects parts of a sentence by referring back to someone or something
Adding ال makes it more specific, like adding 'the' in English

Added است at the beginning to show asking for the action

steps.m: he sought his help (referring to the person who had helped him the day before)

Added Form X pattern (يَسْتَ) to show seeking or requesting help

steps.m: he seeks help from him (the one who sought his help yesterday)

Added fathas to make the basic past tense form before the weak letter change
The و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb
Added fatha to show how to pronounce the letter ل

steps.m: to/for him (the one who asked for help yesterday)
steps.n: Added هُ to show who is being referred to. This connects to the person mentioned earlier who had asked Moses for help the previous day.

steps.m: to/for him (the one who asked for help yesterday)
steps.n: Added the small و mark (ۥ) which helps show how to pronounce the word correctly when reading

This is a proper noun of non-Arabic origin (borrowed from Hebrew/Ancient Egyptian). It doesn't follow typical Arabic root patterns. The name possibly comes from Egyptian meaning 'born of' or 'child of', though some suggest it means 'drawn out of water' in Hebrew.
Added damma (ُ) on م and fatha (َ) on س to show how to pronounce the name correctly
Added alif khanjariya (ٰ) which shows this is a special kind of alif at the end of the name
Added the small alif (ٓ) above to show that the sound is stretched slightly when reading
This particle makes what comes after it stronger or more certain. The shadda (ّ) on the ن makes the emphasis even stronger.
The ك at the end shows this is talking about 'you'. In this ayah, Allah is describing what was said to Musa.

steps.m: to stray from the right path

Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger, showing someone who does this a lot
Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, like saying 'truly' or 'certainly'
Added vowels (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he made clear'
Added مُ at start and ِ in middle to show who/what does the action. This word describes غَوِيٌّ (astray/misguided) in the ayah.
Added emphasis mark (shadda) to show this is a strong quality
This is the base particle meaning 'when' that indicates something happened in the past
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens after something else
The final alif is elongated (آ instead of ا) to emphasize the word and help with pronunciation when stopping at this word

steps.m: that (introduces what he wanted to do: يَبْطِشَ - to strike)

This is the basic meaning showing what someone wants or tries to get
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Adding أَ at start makes it mean 'to cause/make someone want something'
This is a connecting word that joins أَرَادَ (he wanted) with the action يَبْطِشَ (to strike/attack), showing what was wanted
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed to subjunctive mood, often used after 'that' or 'so that'
This is the base relative pronoun used to refer to someone or something
Added ال to make it definite and doubled the ذ letter to connect them smoothly
Added the preposition بِ (with/by) at the beginning. The ا in ال changes to ٱ when connected to بِ

steps.m: he (subject pronoun referring to الَّذِى - the one who)
steps.n: This is a standalone pronoun that shows who is being talked about. In this ayah, it refers back to 'the one who' (الَّذِى) mentioned just before it.
Changes from verb 'to be hostile' into noun 'enemy'
Added shadda and dammatan to show this is someone who is intensely or habitually hostile
Starting with the basic preposition ل which shows direction or purpose
Adding emphasis to the preposition with a shadda
Adding هُمَا which means 'both of them' or 'those two'
Added vowel marks to show past tense and make it mean 'he said'
The middle letter و changes to ا because it comes after fatha - this is a common Arabic spelling rule
Added vowel marks to show proper pronunciation of the name
Added يٰ at the beginning to show we are calling out to Moses directly
Added elongation marks (ٰٓ) at the end to show this is being called out in a strong way
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense and added person marker
Adds أَ at the beginning to make it a question, expressing disapproval in this context
This is a particle that introduces what someone wants/intends to do. Here it connects أَرَادَ (he wanted) to يَبْطِشَ (to strike), showing what was wanted.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Adding تَ at start and changing vowels shows present tense for 'you'
Adding نِى at the end shows that 'me' is receiving the action
This is a comparison particle that shows similarity between things

steps.m: just as (referring to قَتَلْتَ - you killed)
steps.n: Adding ما makes the comparison more specific, referring to the way something was done. Here it refers to how the person killed someone the day before.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: you killed (referring to what Musa did)
steps.n: Added تَ at the end to show 'you' did the action, and changed the last vowel to sukun before the suffix
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to form the basic noun

steps.m: a soul (object of قَتَلْتَ - you killed)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha at the end to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb قَتَلْتَ (you killed)
The base word for 'yesterday'
Added the definite article ال to make it specific
Added بِ at the beginning to show time reference - when something happened
This is a connecting particle that links the verb تُرِيدُ (you want) with what comes after it. It helps express 'you want to...'

Added تُ at beginning to show 'you' are doing the action. In this ayah, it's addressing Musa directly.
This is a basic particle used to exclude what follows it from what precedes it, like saying 'everything but this' or 'all except that'
Added the long alif (آ) at the end to show this word connects to what comes after it. Here it connects to تَكُونَ (you become/are).
This is a particle that connects ideas and shows purpose or result. Here it introduces what someone wants to happen - 'to become' (تَكُونَ).
This is the basic root meaning 'to be' or 'to become'
Added basic vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
Added ت at start to show 'you' and changed middle vowel to show present tense
Added َ (fatha) at the end to show subjunctive mood
The doubled letter shows intensity - someone who uses too much force
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
The doubled ب and added alif show someone who does this action intensely or repeatedly

steps.m: a tyrant (object of تَكُونَ - to become)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha to show this is the object of the verb تَكُونَ (to become) in the ayah
This is a basic preposition showing location or position. In this ayah, it shows location within 'the earth' (الْأَرْضِ).

Added the definite article ال (written as ٱلْ) to specify this particular earth/land

Basic form of the word ما which can mean either 'what' (question word) or 'not' (negation). In this context, it means 'not' because it's negating the verb تُرِيدُ (you want).

The middle letter و changes to ي in the present tense form
تُ at start makes it 'you' in present tense
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of إِن (if/indeed) in this ayah
This is a connecting word that introduces what someone wants or intends to happen. Here it connects تُرِيدُ (you want) with تَكُونَ (you become), showing what is wanted.

Added تَ at the start to show 'you' in present tense

This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what group it belongs to

Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added مُ at start and changed middle vowel to show who does the action
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about many people

steps.m: the people who make things better
