steps.m: to be a man, walk on foot
steps.n: Basic concept of being masculine or walking
The vowels make it into the noun 'man' from the basic concept

steps.m: a man (who came running to warn مُوسَى)
steps.n: Added tanween (double damma) to show this is indefinite - it's talking about some man who came, not a specific known man
This is a basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from, like saying 'from' in English. It's one of the most common Arabic words and isn't built from a root.
Added a shadda (doubled م) because this word connects to the next word أَقْصَا (farthest part). The shadda helps the words flow together smoothly when read.
Basic concept of being distant or far away
The root letters are connected with vowels (َ) and the last letter و changes to ا to make the past tense verb 'he became distant'
Added أ at beginning and sukun on ق to make the superlative form meaning 'farthest'. This word is describing which part of the city (ٱلْمَدِينَةِ) in this ayah.
Basic concept of civilization or urban living
Added vowels to create a noun meaning a place where people settle
Added ة to specify a single city
Added ال to make it specific and ِ to show it belongs to something (in this case, it's part of the phrase 'from the farthest part of the city')

steps.m: to move quickly or strive towards a goal

steps.n: Root letters were connected and vowels added to make past tense 'he' form. The ي changes to ى at the end.
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense, and changed middle vowel to sukun

steps.m: he moves quickly/strives (running to warn مُوسَىٰ)
steps.n: Added alif khanjariyah (ٰ) which is a pronunciation mark showing the long vowel sound. In this ayah, the man is rushing to warn Moses about a plot against him.
Added fathas to make it past tense. This shows the basic form before the weak letter change.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is very common in hollow verbs
This is a proper name borrowed from Hebrew/Egyptian, so it doesn't have an Arabic root
Added diacritical marks to show proper pronunciation
Added يٰ at the beginning to show someone is calling out to Moses. The long alif (ٓ) at the end shows the voice is being stretched out in the call.
This is the basic conditional particle that shows uncertainty about something
Adding shadda changes this from showing uncertainty to showing certainty. In this ayah, it emphasizes that the chiefs are definitely plotting.
Adding vowel marks changes it from a root to a proper verb
Adding the definite article makes it refer to a specific assembly - in this context, it refers to the leaders/nobles who were plotting against Moses
Adding fatha at the end shows this word is the object of إِنَّ (indeed) in the ayah
Added letters to show people doing the action together or among themselves
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they are consulting together (about قَتْل you)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to the chiefs consulting together about killing Prophet Musa.
This is a preposition that shows how something is done or relates to something
Added the pronoun ك (you) to show who the preposition relates to. This word shows who the leaders are plotting about in يَأْتَمِرُونَ (they are plotting).
Changes the root into a present/future tense verb by adding يَ at the start and vowel marks
Changes from singular 'he' to plural 'they' by adding و
Adds كَ to show you are receiving the action (object)

steps.m: in order to kill you (purpose of يَأْتَمِرُونَ - they are plotting)
steps.n: Adds لِ to show this is the purpose or goal of their plotting. This connects to يَأْتَمِرُونَ (they are plotting) in the ayah.

steps.m: to exit or leave a place

steps.n: Adding vowels to make it a command form
Added helper alif to make pronunciation easier

steps.m: so exit! (as a result of what was mentioned)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this command is a result of the previous statement about people plotting to kill Moses
This is a special word that adds emphasis, like saying 'truly' or 'indeed' in English
The ن gets a kasra (ِ) to prepare for connecting with the next letter
Adding ى at the end makes it mean 'I', like saying 'indeed I' instead of just 'indeed'
Added fatha to help with pronunciation

steps.m: for/to you (connected to يَأْتَمِرُونَ - they plot)
steps.n: Added ك with fatha at the end to show this is directed to 'you'. This word connects to يَأْتَمِرُونَ (they plot) to show who they are plotting against.
This is a basic preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or is part of a group. It doesn't come from a root.
Added fatha at the end because the next word starts with ال. This makes it easier to pronounce.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
The verb 'he advised' changes to 'one who advises' by using the pattern فَاعِل (faa3il) which turns verbs into nouns describing who does the action - like changing 'write' to 'writer'
Changed from singular 'adviser' to plural 'advisers' by adding ين at the end
Adding definite article to make the word definite

steps.m: he left (followed by مِنْهَا - from it)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb. This word is followed by مِنْهَا showing what he left from.

steps.m: so he left (followed by مِنْهَا - from it)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this action happened as a result of or following something before it. In this ayah, it shows he left as a result of the previous events.
This is a preposition that shows something coming away from somewhere or something

steps.m: from it/her (referring to the city he left)
steps.n: Added هَا at the end to show what is being moved away from. In this ayah, it refers to the city Moses left.
Changes from verb 'to fear' into the noun 'fear'

steps.m: fearful, being afraid (describes how he left)
steps.n: Changes the word to describe someone who is feeling fear. This describes how Musa was feeling when he left.

steps.m: in a state of fear (describes how he left)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this describes the state he was in while leaving. The آ is just a spelling variation of ا.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Adding تَ at start and shadda in middle changes meaning to 'watching with extra care and attention'
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at the beginning
The ُ shows this is happening now. In this ayah, he is watching carefully because he is afraid, as shown by خَآئِفًا (fearful) in the previous part.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this is past tense
The و (weak letter) changes to ا, a very common change in Arabic verbs

steps.m: Lord (one who nurtures and cares completely)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes this emphasize complete nurturing and care, like how a lord takes full care of those under them
Adding kasra at the end makes this a call/plea to one's Lord, like saying 'O my Lord!'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding shadda (doubling mark) makes the command more emphatic
Added نِى at the end to show who is being saved (me)
Basic preposition showing something comes from somewhere or showing the starting point of something
The sukun changes to fatha when connecting to the next word الْقَوْمِ (the people). This makes it easier to pronounce.
Changed root meaning from 'standing' to mean 'people' - like how a group stands together. This word comes after مِنَ (from) in the phrase 'save me from the wrongdoing people.'
Added ال to make it specific and ِ because it comes after the word مِنَ (from)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the word to show who does the action by adding alif after first letter and kasra after second letter
Changed to plural form by adding ين at the end, and added ال (the) at the beginning
This is a compound particle made up of و (and) + لمّا (when). It's used to connect two events in time.

steps.m: to face or turn towards something

steps.n: Basic vowels (fatha) added to make 'he faced' from root meaning 'to face'
Adding تَ and doubling ج shows he did this action to himself
Adding vowels turns the root into a proper past tense verb
Changed the verb 'to meet' into a word meaning 'direction towards' by adding تِ at start and اء at end
Added long alif and fatha to show this word is describing direction towards Madyan
Adding vowel marks to make the word pronounceable
Changes from verb 'to build city' into name of specific city
Added short vowels (fatha marks) to the root letters to make it past tense meaning 'he said'
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs
Changed from basic root letters to a word meaning 'perhaps/hopefully' by adding vowel marks and ending with a special alif. Used to express hope about what comes next.
Added the small alif (ٰ) above the final letter to show the full pronunciation of the alif maqsurah

steps.m: to care for and help grow
to care for and help grow
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make them pronounceable

steps.m: one who cares for and helps others grow
steps.n: The doubled middle letter (shadda) shows this is someone who really does this action a lot - like a caretaker or guardian
Added ي with kasra to show this is my caretaker. This word is doing the action of عَسَىٰ (meaning 'may/perhaps') in this sentence.
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence
This is a particle that connects to the verb after it (يَهْدِيَنِى) to show what is hoped for or expected. Here it connects 'it may be that' (عَسَىٰ) with 'guides me' (يَهْدِيَنِى)
Changed the root letters into a past tense verb by adding fatha marks (َ) and an alif at the end to mean 'he guided'
Added يَ at start to show present tense, with sukun on ه and kasra on د. The final ي is from the root.
Added نِى at the end to show the action is happening to 'me'
Changes from verb to noun meaning 'state of being equal'
Adds fatha to make the word accusative case, showing it's being used as an object

steps.m: to make a path or way
a path/way that is frequently used
steps.n: The ي makes it mean a specific path rather than just the action of making a path
Added ال to make it definite (the), and ِ at the end shows it's the object being acted upon by the verb يَهْدِيَنِى (guide me) in this ayah
This is a compound particle made up of و (and) + لما (when). The shadda on ل is an inherent part of لمّا, not a derivational change.

steps.m: to arrive at or reach a place (water)
he arrived (at مَآءَ مَدْيَنَ - the water of Madyan)
steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word tells us what 'he' (Prophet Musa) did when he reached the water of Madyan.
This is the basic root that refers to water or liquid substances
Connected the root letters م-و-ه with short vowel marks (fatHa) to make it pronounceable
The middle letter و becomes ا and combines with final ه to make hamza ء - this is a common change with weak letters

steps.m: water (object of وَرَدَ - he came to)
steps.n: Added the long alif (آ) to show extended pronunciation and fatha at the end to show it's the object of the verb وَرَدَ (he came to)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic noun
Adds ي to make it a specific place name
Adds a fatha mark at the end to make it a proper noun

steps.m: he found (subject of يَسْقُونَ - they were watering)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word is part of the story describing what Musa found at the well.
Basic preposition meaning 'on' or 'upon'
The ى changes to ي with sukun when adding a suffix
Added هِ at the end to mean 'him'. This word connects to وَجَدَ (he found) in the ayah, showing who was found.
The ُ (damma) makes it passive/receptive
Changed from plural 'nations' to singular 'nation' by adding fatha and taa marboota - like going from 'groups' to 'group'

steps.m: a community/nation (that he found at the water of Madyan)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha to show this is the object of وَجَدَ (he found) in the ayah
Added fatha at the end because it connects to the next word ٱلنَّاسِ (the people). This is like adding glue between words to make them flow better when speaking.
Added shadda (doubled م) to emphasize the word and make it stronger in pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning, it just makes it sound stronger.

steps.m: to be social or friendly with others
people (those who are social with each other)
steps.n: Changed from a root meaning 'to be social' into a noun meaning 'people' by adding a long 'aa' sound in the middle
Added 'the' at the beginning and doubled the ن letter to help pronunciation
Added kasra at the end because this word comes after مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah
Connected root letters with fatha marks. The ي changes to ى because it's at the end of the word.
Adding يَ at start and changing vowels shows this is happening now or in the future. The ى changes back to ي.
Adding ون shows multiple men are doing the action. The ي at the end is dropped before adding ون.
Added fatha vowels to make a past tense verb meaning 'he found'
Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and'
Basic preposition showing that the group (أُمَّةً) came from or was part of the people (ٱلنَّاسِ). In this ayah, it describes finding a group from among the people at the water.
Adding damma makes it into a word meaning 'below'
Added kasra to show this connects to what comes after it

steps.m: besides them (referring to the people who were watering)
steps.n: Added هِمُ to show this refers to the group of people mentioned before (those who were watering)
Changed root word for 'person' into feminine form by adding special endings to make it mean 'woman'
Changes from dual form (two women) to single form (one woman) by removing dual ending -ayn
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he drove away' to a noun meaning 'the act of driving away'
Added تَ prefix to show feminine subject in present tense
Added انِ suffix to show two feminine subjects. This refers to the two women mentioned in the ayah who are keeping their flocks back from the water.
Added vowel marks (fathas) to the root letters to make it mean 'he said' in the past

steps.m: he said (asking about مَا خَطْبُكُمَا - what is your situation?)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic for hollow verbs. This word is part of Musa's question to the two women about their situation.
This is an interrogative pronoun used by Musa to ask the two women about their situation. In this context, it's part of the phrase مَا خَطْبُكُمَا meaning 'what is your matter/situation?'

steps.m: to speak about a matter or concern

steps.n: Root letters turned into a noun meaning 'matter' by adding vowel marks (fatha-sukun pattern)
Added damma to show this is the subject being discussed
Added كُمَا to show the matter belongs to both people being addressed
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense form
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This makes it easier to pronounce.
Added ت with sukun at the end to show a female is doing the action. This matches with امْرَأَتَيْنِ (two women) mentioned in the ayah.

steps.m: they both (f) said (referring to امْرَأَتَيْنِ - the two women)
steps.n: Changed تْ to تَا to show exactly two females are speaking. This refers to the two women (امْرَأَتَيْنِ) who were keeping back their flocks.
This is a negative particle that tells us the women are NOT watering their flocks. It directly negates the verb نَسْقِى (we water) that comes after it in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The ي changes to ى because it's at the end of the word.

Added نَ at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action. The middle letter gets a sukun (سْ) and the last root letter gets a kasra (قِ) in this form. This word relates to the context where two women are explaining that they give water to their flocks.
Basic form of the preposition that shows a limit or endpoint
Added ىٰ (alif maqsurah) at the end to show this connects to the next word. Here it connects to يُصْدِرَ (depart) to mean 'until they depart'
Added basic past tense vowels
Added hamza at beginning to make the action causative - showing someone making something happen

steps.m: he makes (something) return (until the shepherds finish)
steps.n: Changed from past to present tense because it comes after 'until' (حَتَّىٰ) to show something that hasn't happened yet

steps.m: to take care of animals by feeding them

steps.n: Changes the word pattern to make it plural, showing many shepherds instead of just one
Makes the noun definite
Shows this word is the subject of the sentence

steps.m: to nurture/care for as a father

steps.n: The vowels َ and ُ make this a noun meaning father
Added نَا at the end to show possession. This word is the subject in the sentence 'our father is an elderly man', where شَيْخٌ كَبِيرٌ (elderly man) describes him.

steps.m: and our father (subject of شَيْخٌ كَبِيرٌ)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create a noun meaning 'old man' from the concept of aging
Added tanween damma to show this is describing 'our father' (أَبُونَا) in the sentence
Added vowels and ي to make an adjective meaning 'old/elderly'. This word describes أَبُونَا (our father) in this ayah.
Added tanween (double damma) to show this is a complete description

steps.m: to give drink or water to someone/something

steps.n: The root letters got vowels (fathas) and since it's past tense, the last letter became ى (alif maqsurah)
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened after something else mentioned before
Added small alif (ٰ) after ى to help with pronunciation, but doesn't change the meaning
Added fatha to show the vowel sound on the letter lam
Added هُ (him/it) to show who the action is for

steps.m: for both of them (referring to the two women he watered for)
steps.n: Added ما to make it refer to two people instead of one. This relates to the two women mentioned in the previous part of the verse (فَسَقَىٰ - 'so he watered')
Basic connecting word that shows one thing follows another after a gap in time

Added damma (ُ) and shadda with fatha (َّ) to complete the word. This connects the action of watering (فَسَقَىٰ) to the next action of turning (تَوَلَّىٰٓ), showing they happened in sequence with some time between them.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added emphasis by doubling the middle letter
Added تَ at beginning to show he did this action to himself
Added ى at the end to show this is a past tense verb ending in a weak letter
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or movement towards something. Here it shows Moses went towards the shade.
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation: kasra (ِ) under إ and fatha (َ) over ل
Root meaning 'shade' becomes a simple noun by adding i-vowel and doubling the last letter
Added ٱل to make it specific and ِ at the end because it follows إِلَى (to)
Added short vowels 'a' to make it mean 'he said' in the past
Changed middle weak letter و to ا, which is common in hollow verbs

steps.m: and then he said (after سَقَىٰ/he gave water)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happened after giving water to their animals
Added fatha and doubled the middle letter to create a noun meaning 'one who does this action perfectly'
Added kasra at the end to show possession (my) and direct address (O)
This is the basic form of the word that adds emphasis to what comes after it, like saying 'truly' or 'certainly' in English
Added ي at the end to show this is about myself ('I'). The نّ keeps its double (shadda) to maintain the emphasis.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't change the meaning.
Basic interrogative/relative pronoun meaning 'what'

steps.m: for what (relates to فَقِيرٌ - in need)
steps.n: Added لِ at beginning to show purpose or reason. In this ayah, it connects to فَقِيرٌ (in need) to express 'in need of what'

steps.m: for what (relates to فَقِيرٌ - in need)
steps.n: Added alif at end to lengthen the vowel sound. This is just a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning and sukun on ن makes it mean 'causing something to descend'
Adding تَ changes the subject to 'you' (masculine singular). The sukun moves to the ل
This is the basic preposition meaning 'to' or 'towards'

steps.m: to me (connected to فَقِيرٌ - in need)
steps.n: Added ىَّ (with fatha and shadda) at the end to show this means 'to me'. This word connects to فَقِيرٌ (in need) to express 'I am in need of what you send down'
Basic particle showing starting point, source, or origin
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation. This word connects to خَيْرٍ (good/goodness) to show 'from goodness'
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to make it a noun meaning 'good thing'

steps.m: any good thing (that You send down to me)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra at the end to show this is referring to any good thing. In this ayah, it's specifically referring to any good thing that Allah might send down.

steps.m: to lack something, to be in need

steps.n: Root letters connected with vowels to make an adjective describing someone who is poor/needy
Added ي to show this describes someone who has this quality as a characteristic
Added ٌ (tanween) to show this word is describing someone as poor/needy
Basic meaning of coming or arriving
Changed from basic root letters to past tense 'he came' by adding vowels and turning middle ي into ا
Added extra alif to emphasize the action of coming
Added ت with sukun to show feminine subject
Added فَ at beginning to show sequence of events

steps.m: and then she came to him
steps.n: Added هُ at end to show who she came to
Basic concept of oneness or unity
Changes to feminine form by adding ى at the end
Small alif added above the ى to help with pronunciation

steps.m: one of them both (referring to the two daughters)
steps.n: Added هُمَا at the end to show this refers to two people (the two daughters mentioned in the story)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: she walks (approaching the speaker in إِحْدَىٰهُمَا)
steps.n: Changed to feminine by replacing يَ with تَ. This describes one of the two daughters (إِحْدَىٰهُمَا) walking towards Moses.
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or above something else. Here it connects to the verb تَمْشِى (she was walking) to show how she came - with shyness upon her.

steps.m: to be shy, to feel bashful
to act shyly, to show shyness
steps.n: Added prefix that shows doing the action to oneself or seeking the quality
Changes the verb into a noun describing the state or quality of being shy
Added tanween kasra to show this describes how the action تَمْشِى (she walks) is done
Adding vowel marks to make basic past tense verb pattern

steps.m: she said (subject of تَمْشِى - she walks)
steps.n: Changed the middle letter و to ا and added ت at the end to show a female did the action. This word connects to تَمْشِى (she walks) in the ayah, showing both actions are done by the same woman.
This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger, like adding an exclamation mark in English. Here it makes the girl's statement stronger.
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter. The و drops because it's a weak letter at the end.
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by someone (will be specified in next step)
Added ى to show the father belongs to me. This word is the subject (doer) of يَدْعُوكَ (calls you) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end.
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed middle letter's mark to sukun, and final letter to damma with و to show ongoing action

steps.m: he calls/invites you (object of father's calling)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show that 'you' is receiving the action. In this ayah, the father (أب) is doing the calling.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: he gives/will give what is deserved
steps.n: Adding يَ at the start and changing the middle vowel to ِ (kasra) changes it from past tense to present/future tense

steps.m: so that he may give what is deserved (purpose of يَدْعُوكَ - he calls you)
steps.n: Added ل at the beginning to indicate purpose/intent ('so that')

steps.m: so that he may give you what you deserve (purpose of يَدْعُوكَ - he calls you)
steps.n: Added ك ('you') at the end to show who is receiving the reward. Connected to يدعوك (he calls you) to show why he calls.

steps.m: to reward or compensate for work

steps.n: Changed from action to the thing itself (the payment/reward). This is what's being given in this ayah.
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object receiving the action in the sentence (what is being given to you)
This is a basic particle meaning 'what' or 'that which'. It connects to يَدْعُوكَ (he calls you) to show what he's calling about.
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it past tense 'he gave water'. The ي at the end becomes ى when writing the past tense.
Added تَ at the end to show 'you' did the action. This word connects to لَنَا (to us) in the ayah.
