Added وا at the end to show that a group of people (they) did the action
Added vowel marks to create the basic noun form for magic or sorcery

steps.m: two magics (referring to Torah and Quran that the disbelievers called magic)
steps.n: Added انِ to show exactly two things. In this ayah, it refers to two divine books that the disbelievers called magic.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Adding alif makes it mean doing something together with someone else (mutual action)
Adding تَ shows the action is reciprocal - done between two parties to each other

steps.m: they both supported each other (referring to سِحْرَانِ - two magics)
steps.n: Adding alif at the end shows this refers to exactly two things (the two magics mentioned)
Added vowels to make past tense. The middle و changes to ا in this form.
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action
Added وَ at beginning to mean 'and'. The ٓ and ۟ are pronunciation marks that don't change the meaning.
This is a special word that adds emphasis to what comes after it, like saying 'truly' or 'certainly' at the start of a sentence
Adding ا at the end changes this from just emphasis to emphasis specifically about 'we/us'

steps.m: to be complete, whole, or all-encompassing

steps.n: The root letters combine with a shadda (doubled middle letter) to make a word meaning 'every' or 'all'
The two dots under (ٍ) show this word is being used after a preposition like 'bi' (with/by)

steps.m: with each (of them) (كافِرُونَ follows this word)
steps.n: The ب means 'with' or 'in'. This word connects to كافِرُونَ (disbelievers) which comes after it in the ayah
Added ا after first letter and vowels to show who does the action. This creates the doer form, like changing 'reject' to 'rejecter' in English.

steps.m: those who reject/deny truth (predicate of إِنَّا)
steps.n: Added ون at the end to make it plural (more than two). This word describes what 'we' are after إِنَّا (indeed we).
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Changed from 'he said' to 'say!' as a command. The middle letter و disappeared to make it shorter and easier to say - this happens a lot with commands in Arabic that have و in the middle.
Connected the root letters together to form the base word
Added فَ to show this is a response or result (in this ayah, it's responding to the previous statement)
Changed from regular verb to command form by dropping the last letter and adding a stop mark (sukun) and 'u' sound (damma)
Added وا to make it a command for multiple people (in this ayah, addressing those who deny the truth)
Added basic past tense form by connecting letters with fatha
Changes from verb 'wrote' to noun 'book' by adding alif

steps.m: with a book (object they are challenged to bring)
steps.n: The letter ب (bi) was added at the start to mean 'with', and the word got a special ending mark (tanween) to show it's 'a' book, not 'the' book

steps.m: from (connects عِندِ with what comes before)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects 'from the presence of' (مِنْ عِندِ) to what comes before.

steps.m: from (connects عِندِ with what comes before)
steps.n: Added a shadda (doubling mark) on the م because it comes after a word ending in a vowel sound. This helps connect the words smoothly when reading.
Added the basic vowel pattern that makes this root into a noun meaning 'presence'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it (اللَّهِ). Together they mean 'from Allah's presence'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns

This is the unique name for the one true God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the shadda emphasizes it, and the kasra at the end shows it belongs to or connects with something else in the sentence.

steps.m: he (referring to the book mentioned in بِكِتٰبٍ)
steps.n: This is a basic pronoun used to refer back to the book (كِتٰبٍ) mentioned earlier in the ayah. The هُ shows it's masculine (matching كِتٰبٍ) and the وَ shows it's independent (not attached to another word).

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Added vowel marks (fatha) to make 'he guided'. The ي at the end becomes ى when it's the last letter.

steps.m: he caused to be guided/he made guide
steps.n: Adding أ at start changes meaning to 'cause someone to do the action'

steps.m: he caused to be guided/he made guide
steps.n: Adding alif maqsurah (ى with small alif) at end shows this is past tense
This is the basic preposition that shows something coming from somewhere or someone
Added the dual pronoun هُمَا to show this refers to two people or things
The alif at the end is written with a special mark (آ) but the meaning stays the same
Adding vowels turns the root into a complete past tense verb meaning 'to follow'
Added اِتّ at the start to show following something with more effort and purpose
Changed from 'he followed' to 'you follow!' by changing the vowels to make it a command to one person
Changed from a command (follow!) to 'I will follow' by changing the first vowel
Added هُ to show who is being followed

steps.m: if (introduces condition for كُنتُمْ صٰدِقِينَ - you are truthful)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it connects 'bring a book' with 'if you are truthful', showing that bringing the book would prove their truthfulness.
Added vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he was'
The middle weak letter و drops out, and the vowels change to match the common pattern for this type of verb
Adding تُمْ at the end changes the meaning to 'you all' in the past tense
Added ا after first letter to show who does the action, like changing 'tell truth' to 'truth-teller'
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people who tell the truth
Changed the regular alif to a small alif above the ص and added a fatha mark at the end because this word is the object (thing being acted upon) in the sentence
This is a basic conditional particle that introduces a condition, similar to saying 'if' in English. In this ayah, it introduces the condition 'if they do not respond to you'.
Added فَ at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it, showing sequence or result. It's like saying 'then if' or 'and if' in English.
This is a negative particle that changes the meaning of the verb يَسْتَجِيبُوا۟ (they respond) to 'they did not respond' in the past tense
Added shadda on ل for emphasis and stronger pronunciation

steps.m: to travel through, traverse (which led to meaning of going back and forth in conversation)

steps.n: Added vowels (fatha) between root letters to make past tense verb meaning 'he traveled'
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at the start. The middle letter changes to ي to make it easier to say.
Added است to show actively seeking or trying to do the action
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action. This verb connects to لَكَ (to you) in the ayah.
Added fatha to show this means 'to/for'

steps.m: to/for you (responding to يَسْتَجِيبُوا - they respond)
steps.n: Added ك with fatha at the end to show this is directed 'to you'. This word shows who they are responding to in يَسْتَجِيبُوا (they respond).
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to know'
Changed from 'he knew' to 'know!' by adding اِ at start and making it a command (putting sukun on middle letter)
Added فَ at beginning to show this follows from what came before
This is a compound particle made up of two parts: أَنَّ which means 'that' and ما which adds the meaning of 'only' or 'merely'. Together they emphasize that something is limited or restricted.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create basic past tense verb
Changes from past tense (he followed) to present tense (he follows) by adding يَ at the start
Intensifies meaning through letter doubling

steps.m: they follow eagerly/deliberately (subject of فَإِن لَّمْ يَسْتَجِيبُوا)
steps.n: Changed from 'he follows' to 'they follow' to show what multiple people are doing (plural form)

steps.m: to desire or follow one's inclinations

steps.n: Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
Changes from a verb (to desire) into a plural noun (multiple desires) - like going from 'want' to 'wants'
Shows this word is being acted upon by يَتَّبِعُونَ (they follow)

steps.m: their desires (as object of يَتَّبِعُونَ - they follow)
steps.n: Shows these desires belong to them
This is a relative pronoun used to ask about or refer to people
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before. This word is the subject of أَضَلُّ (more astray) in this ayah.

steps.m: to stray/deviate from the right path

steps.n: Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
Added أَ at beginning and ُ at end to make it comparative, showing something is 'more' than something else. In this ayah, it's comparing to مَنِ (whoever).
Basic interrogative/relative pronoun meaning 'who' or 'whoever'
Added preposition مِن before مَن to show source or origin
The ن of مِن combines with the م of مَن, shown by the shadda mark (ّ)
Added kasra at the end to show this is the subject of the verb اتَّبَعَ (followed) in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: he followed intensively (by his own choice)
steps.n: Added اِتْ at the start to show he chose to follow with extra effort

steps.m: he followed intensively (by his own choice)
steps.n: The two ت letters combine into one strong ت with shadda

steps.m: to desire strongly/to follow one's whims

steps.n: Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
Added alif khanjariya (small alif) above the ى to show it's pronounced as a long vowel
Added pronoun هُ meaning 'his'. This word is being acted upon by the verb اتَّبَعَ (followed) in the ayah.
This root carries the basic idea of difference or change
The root letters get vowels (َ and ْ) to make a word meaning 'other than'
The بِ at the beginning means 'with/by' and the ِ at the end shows this word connects to هُدًى (guidance) that comes after it
Added damma and sukun to create a noun from the root meaning
Changed the ending to make it mean 'guidance' in general (not specific guidance). The word follows بِغَيْرِ which means 'without'.
Basic preposition meaning 'from'. This word doesn't come from a root - it's a basic building block of Arabic.
The م gets doubled (gets a shadda) because it comes right after another word ending in ل
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly to the next word اللَّهِ (Allah)
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' by adding vowel marks
Changed from the general word for 'god' to become Allah (God's name) by adding ال and combining letters
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after مِنَ
Basic conditional particle that shows something depends on something else

steps.m: indeed/verily (emphasizes that اللَّهَ does not guide)
steps.n: Adding shadda changes this from 'if' to a strong emphasis particle. Here it emphasizes that Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.
Changed the root into the basic noun form for 'a deity'
Added ال and merged letters to create the unique name for The One God. The hamza is dropped and the lam gets a shadda.
Added fatha at the end because this word is the object of إِنَّ (indeed). Also changed alif to wasl alif for pronunciation.
Basic negative particle that negates the verb يَهْدِى (guides) that comes after it, changing its meaning to 'does not guide'
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb form
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense, changed middle vowel to kasra, and added sukun after first letter for present tense pattern
Added ى at the end to make it a present tense verb. This is a common ending for verbs ending in weak letters.
Changes verb into noun for a group that establishes/maintains something. This word is being acted upon by يَهْدِى (guides) in this ayah.

steps.m: the people (being acted upon by يَهْدِى - guides)
steps.n: Added 'al' (ال) at start to mean 'the' and fatha at end because the people are being guided (they receive the action)
This pattern shows someone who does the action regularly or as a characteristic - like adding '-er' in English (teach → teacher)
Makes the word plural to show multiple wrongdoers. This word is being acted upon by the verb يهدي (guides) - Allah does not guide them.
Adds 'the' to show we're talking about specific wrongdoers

steps.m: the wrongdoers/oppressors (who Allah does not guide)
steps.n: The doubled letter adds emphasis to show these are truly/definitely wrongdoers. This is the object of يهدي (guides) - showing who Allah does not guide.
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation of this particle that shows certainty
Added لَ at the beginning to add extra emphasis, like saying 'most certainly' instead of just 'certainly'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the action more intense or repeated
Adding نا shows that 'we' did the action. This connects to لَهُمُ (to them) in the ayah.
Added fatha to show this is a preposition meaning 'to' or 'for'
Added هُمُ to show who the action is for. This word connects to يَتَذَكَّرُونَ (they remember) in this ayah, showing who might remember.
Added fatha and sukun to create the noun form meaning 'speech' or 'saying'
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific saying or speech

steps.m: the saying, the speech (object of وَصَّلْنَا - we have conveyed)
steps.n: Adding fatha at the end shows this word is the object being acted upon by the verb وَصَّلْنَا (we have conveyed)
This is a basic particle that shows hope or expectation for something to happen. It's like saying 'hopefully' or 'maybe' in English.

steps.m: so that they might/perhaps they will (connected to يَتَذَكَّرُونَ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who the hope or expectation is about. This word connects to يَتَذَكَّرُونَ (they remember/take heed) to show hope that they will remember or take heed.
The root becomes a noun meaning 'remembrance' or 'mention'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the meaning more intense or repetitive
Adding تَ shows the person is doing the action to themselves
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ at end
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that means 'who'
Added ين to make it refer to multiple people
Added ال at the beginning to make it more specific, like saying 'those who' instead of just 'who'
Added final touches: 1) Changed ا to ٱ at start, 2) Added shadda on ل, 3) Added َ at end. This word connects to يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe) in this ayah.

steps.m: to bring or come with something
steps.n: The weak letter ي at the end affects how the word is formed
Changed the root into Form IV to show giving or bringing something to someone
Changed the final ى to يْ to prepare for attachments
Added نٰ to show 'we' did the action
Added هُمُ to show who received the action. This word connects to كِتٰبَ (book) which comes after it in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create basic past tense verb
Transforms from basic verb to noun indicating result/object of writing. This word is in accusative case because it is the object (thing being given) in the phrase 'We gave them the Book'.
Adding definite article makes the noun definite
Changed to accusative case with fatha ending because it's the object (thing being given) in 'We gave them the Book'. Also uses ٱ instead of اَ and ٰ instead of ا as common spelling variations.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from
Added the kasra (ِ) under م to show how to pronounce it correctly. In this ayah, it comes before the word قَبْلِهِۦ (before it) to show the starting point in time.
Added fatha and sukun to connect the letters and make it mean 'before' as a concept
Added kasra because it follows the word مِن (from)
Added هِۦ to show this is happening before the Book (ٱلْكِتٰبَ)

This is a standalone pronoun meaning 'they' (for a group of males or mixed gender). In this ayah, it refers to the people who were given the Book (ٱلَّذِينَ ءَاتَيْنٰهُمُ ٱلْكِتٰبَ) and is the subject of the verb يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe).
Added kasra under ب to show it's a preposition meaning with/by
Added ه with kasra to show this means 'it/him'. This connects to the verb يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe), showing what they believe in.
Added small alif after ه to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
This is the basic past tense form of the verb
Adding vowel marks to show it's a past tense feeling verb
Added an alif at the start to make it mean 'actively choosing to believe' instead of just 'feeling safe'
Changed to present tense by adding يُ prefix and adjusting vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
This is a compound particle made up of 'wa' (and) plus 'idha' (when)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end.
Changed to passive voice by adding يُ at start and changing vowels. Shows something is being recited rather than someone doing the reciting.
Added alif khanjariya (ٰ) which is just a writing style variation. This word is connected to عَلَيْهِمْ (upon them) in the ayah.
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation of basic preposition
Changed ى to يْ to prepare for attaching the pronoun. This is a spelling change that happens when adding suffixes.
Added هِمْ at the end to show this applies to 'them' (a group of people)
Adding the basic vowel pattern for past tense verbs

steps.m: they said (subject of ءَامَنَّا - they believed)
steps.n: Changed middle letter و to ا and added وا at the end to show a group of people said something. Links to ءَامَنَّا (they believed) which comes next.
Added optional marks (small و above alif and small circle) that don't change meaning
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb about feeling secure
Changed from basic 'trust' to stronger religious 'belief' by adding alif - like upgrading from just feeling safe to having deep faith
Added shadda to emphasize the action

steps.m: we believed (subject of قالوا - they said)
steps.n: Added نا at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. This word tells what 'they said' in the ayah.
Third person masculine singular pronoun that refers back to the Quran being recited
Preposition بِ (in/with) attached to pronoun
Added pronunciation helpers: ۦ shows the 'hi' sound clearly and ٓ extends the sound slightly
This is an emphatic particle that makes what comes after it stronger or more certain, like adding 'indeed' in English

steps.m: indeed it/he (referring to what was recited to them)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show what 'indeed' is referring to. Here it refers back to what was recited to them (يُتْلَىٰ) in the ayah.
The shadda (doubled letter) makes it stronger, showing this is absolute or complete truth
Adding ال makes it specific - this is THE truth. This word explains what 'it' (هُ) in إِنَّهُ (indeed it) is referring to
The ُ ending shows this word completes the meaning of إِنَّهُ (indeed it), telling us what 'it' is
This is a basic preposition showing where something comes from or starts. In this ayah, it combines with قَبْلِهِۦ (before it) to show timing - 'from before it'
The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures completely and perfectly
The shadda (double letter mark) makes the 'R' sound stronger to show importance
The kasra shows this word is ready to be possessed by something

steps.m: our Lord (in this ayah: speaking to Allah)
steps.n: Adding نَآ shows this is 'our Lord'. The long alif at the end shows emphasis in calling out to Allah
This is an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows
Added نا suffix as 'we' pronoun. This word connects to كُنَّا (we were) in this ayah, showing who was being Muslim before.
Changed from root 'to be' into 'he was' by dropping middle letter و and adding past tense pattern

steps.m: from (the truth is from رَّبِّنَآ our Lord)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows something comes from a source. In this ayah, it shows the truth comes from our Lord.
The root 'to come before' becomes 'before' when مِن owns it, making it end with kasra
Added هِ meaning 'his/its', with ۦ to help pronunciation

steps.m: to submit, surrender, or be at peace

steps.n: Added مُ at the start to show who does the action, and ِ in the middle to complete the pattern
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about many people

steps.m: Muslims (as the object of كُنَّا - we were)
steps.n: Added َ at the end to show this word is being acted upon by كُنَّا (we were) in the ayah
This is the base form meaning 'those' - it's a demonstrative pronoun used to point to things or people
The alif with line (ٰ) extends the sound to show distance - like saying 'those over there'
The ئِكَ ending specifies that we're pointing to people rather than things, and makes it more formal
The يُ at the start shows someone else is doing the action to 'him'. The hamza (ء) moves after يُ because of pronunciation rules.

steps.m: they are given (passive, referring to أُو۟لٰٓئِكَ - those)
steps.n: Added plural masculine ending ون to show multiple recipients. This matches with أُو۟لٰٓئِكَ (those) who are the subjects receiving the action.
Root letters أجر became a noun meaning 'reward' by adding the vowels َ and ْ
Added fatha at the end because this word is receiving the action of being given (they are given their reward)
Added هُم at the end to show the reward belongs to them. This whole phrase is what's being given in the ayah (they are given their reward)
Adding vowel marks makes this a past tense verb
Changed the verb 'pass by' into a noun meaning 'one time' by doubling the middle letter and adding ة at the end
Added dual ending to show exactly two times. This word is the object showing what they will be given twice in the phrase 'they will be given their reward twice'
Doubles the first letter م to add emphasis to 'two times'
Added kasra (ِ) to show this is a preposition meaning with/by/because of

steps.m: because of what (connects to صَبَرُوا - they were patient)
steps.n: Added ما which means 'what' to create the meaning 'because of what'. This connects to the next word صَبَرُوا (they were patient) to mean 'because of what they patiently endured'
Added vowel marks to make it 'he was patient' in the past tense
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create past tense verb
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show ongoing action
Added ء to properly write the hamza sound
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
Added و at start to connect with previous phrase. In this ayah, they push away evil with good deeds (الْحَسَنَةِ)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic adjective

steps.m: a good deed (one instance of goodness)
steps.n: Forms feminine noun indicating one occurrence of the quality of goodness
Adds 'the' (ال) to make it specific

steps.m: with the good deed (contrasted with ٱلسَّيِّئَةَ/evil deed)
steps.n: Added preposition 'bi' meaning 'with' and kasra at the end to show connection. This word is contrasted with 'evil deed' in the ayah.
The و changes to ي and gets doubled to show something is very evil or harmful
Adding ة makes it refer to one specific evil action instead of evil in general
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific evil deed that was mentioned or known. The س gets doubled (شدة) because it comes after ال

steps.m: the evil deed (being pushed away by يَدْرَءُونَ)

The preposition مِن assimilates with ما, doubling the م

steps.m: to provide with what is needed

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added نا to show 'we' did the action, and added sukun before it
Added هُمْ (hum) meaning 'them' at the end to show who received the provision
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing middle vowel to kasra
Added ون at the end to show multiple people doing the action
This is a conditional particle that introduces a time condition, similar to saying 'at the time when' or 'whenever' in English

steps.m: and when (connects to سَمِعُوا۟ - they heard)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it. This word introduces what happens when they hear idle talk.
Adding vowels turns the root into a complete past tense verb meaning 'to hear'
Changed from one person to many people by adding وا at the end. This word connects to أَعْرَضُوا۟ (they turned away) in the ayah, showing the same group doing both actions.

steps.m: to speak without purpose or benefit

steps.n: Root letters got vowels (َ and ْ) to make a noun meaning 'empty/useless speech'
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific
Added َ at the end to show this is the object of the verb 'they turned away from' (أَعْرَضُوا۟)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added hamza with fatha at start and sukun on first root letter to make the meaning more active/intentional - 'deliberately turn away' rather than just 'turn'
Added وا with damma before it to show multiple people did this action. The small mark after وا (۟) is just for pronunciation.
Basic preposition meaning 'away from' or 'from'. In this ayah, it's used with أَعْرَضُوا۟ (they turned away) to show what they turned away from.

steps.m: away from it/him (referring to ٱللَّغْوَ - vain talk)
steps.n: Added pronoun هُ (him/it) to show what they turned away from. Here it refers back to ٱللَّغْوَ (vain talk) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Added fathas to make it past tense. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas - this is a common pattern in Arabic.
Added وا at the end to show a group of people did the action

steps.m: and they said (connecting to what came before)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this with the previous part where they turned away from vain talk (أَعْرَضُوا۟)
Added fatha to the letter ل to show its pronunciation
Added نا at the end which means 'us' or 'our', making the word mean 'for us'
Added elongation mark (ـآ) to show the extended pronunciation of the final alif. This word is connected to أَعْمٰلُنَا (our deeds) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
Changes from single action to plural form meaning multiple deeds/actions
Adds 'our' and shows this is the subject (doer) in the phrase 'for us are our deeds' (لَنَا أَعْمٰلُنَا)
Starting with the basic preposition that shows direction or purpose
Added the plural 'you' pronoun to show who is being addressed

steps.m: and for you all (connected to لَنَآ أَعْمٰلُنَا - 'for us are our deeds')
steps.n: Added the conjunction 'wa' to connect this phrase with the previous statement about 'our deeds', showing the parallel structure between what belongs to 'us' and what belongs to 'you all'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic noun for one action
Changes the word to show many deeds instead of just one
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) in this style of writing, but means the same thing
Adds a damma mark to show this is the main subject
Adds كُمْ to show these deeds belong to multiple people being spoken to
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic noun form
Adding alif makes it a noun meaning a state of peace
Adding tanween makes it an indefinite noun meaning 'a peace'
The ى changes to ي with sukun when connecting to a suffix. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect meaning.
Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to
This is a negative particle that negates the verb نَبْتَغِى (we seek) that comes after it, making the meaning 'we do not seek'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Added نَ at beginning to show 'we' are doing the action, changed vowels to show present tense
Added ت between first and second root letters to show the action is done for oneself
Added ن at start to show 'we' are doing the action
Adding alif after first root letter to show who does the action. This pattern shows who or what has a quality.
Adding masculine plural ending ين. This word is the object (what is being sought) of the verb نَبْتَغِى (we seek).

steps.m: the ignorant ones (object of نَبْتَغِى/we seek)
steps.n: Adding the definite article ال and the fatha at the end shows this is the object (thing being acted upon) in relation to the verb نَبْتَغِى (we seek) in this ayah
This is an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows. In this ayah, it emphasizes that 'you' cannot guide whom you love.

steps.m: indeed you (subject of لَا تَهْدِى/do not guide)
steps.n: The كَ suffix shows this is addressed to 'you' (masculine singular). In this ayah, it's addressing the Prophet ﷺ, telling him he cannot guide whom he loves.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb تَهْدِى (you guide) negative, changing its meaning to 'you do not guide'
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Added تَ at start to show 'you', changed middle vowel to sukun, and last vowel to kasra to show present tense. This is addressing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ in the context of the ayah.
This is a relative pronoun used to refer to people. In this ayah, it refers to the person that you (the Prophet) want to guide.
Added hamza at beginning to show causing someone else to do the action
Added تَ at the end to change from 'he made love' to 'you loved' - showing you (as a male) did this action in the past
This is the basic form of the word meaning 'but'. The alif after ل is written as a long alif (لٰ) in the Uthmani script.
Adding shadda with fatha (نَّ) makes the meaning stronger and more emphatic

steps.m: and yet/however (connecting to إِنَّكَ لَا تَهْدِى مَنْ أَحْبَبْتَ)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this word to the previous statement 'Indeed, you do not guide whom you like.' The و here shows contrast between ideas.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows the ل is doubled. This is a unique word for the One True God.

steps.m: Allah (as the object of يَهْدِى - guides)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being acted upon by the verb يَهْدِى (guides). Also changed initial alif to wasl form since it connects to previous word.

steps.m: to guide (to show the right path)

steps.n: Changed the root letters into a past tense verb by adding the vowel marks 'fatha' (َ) to mean 'he guided'
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. This shows Allah's ongoing guidance.
Adding ى at the end is just a spelling variation of the same word
This is a relative pronoun used to refer to people. In this ayah, it refers to the person whom the Prophet ﷺ wanted to guide. It's the object (receiver) of the verb تَهْدِى (you guide).
The root letters got vowels (fathas) and the middle letter ي became ا to make 'he wanted'
Added يَ at start to show present tense and ُ at end to show he is doing the action. This word is connected to يَهْدِى (he guides) in the ayah.
Added long ا to show the action takes time or is ongoing
Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce the word - هُ shows 'hu' sound and وَ shows 'wa' sound
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This word is the subject (doer) of أَعْلَمُ (most knowing) in this ayah.
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to comparative/superlative form by adding أَ at start, putting sukun on first root letter, and adding ُ at end. This word relates to بِٱلْمُهْتَدِينَ (the guided ones) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels. The final ي becomes ى because it's a weak letter at the end.
Changes from 'he guided' to 'someone who is guided' - like changing from the doer to the receiver of guidance
The ينَ ending shows this is plural and is the object of the verb أَعْلَمُ (most knowing) in this ayah
Definite article makes the noun definite

steps.m: with/about the guided ones (object of أَعْلَمُ)
steps.n: The بِ shows Allah is most knowing 'about/with regard to' the guided ones. This whole phrase is the object of أَعْلَمُ (most knowing) in this ayah.
The middle letter و changes to ا when we add the past tense pattern. This is a common change with middle-weak verbs.
Adding وا at the end changes 'he' to 'they' and adds ُ before it
Adding وَ at the beginning connects this word to what came before it
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it connects two parts: the condition (following the guidance) and its result (being snatched away from the land).
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added ن at start to show 'we' and doubled the ت with shadda to show doing something closely or intensely
Added kasra at the end to show this is in jussive mood (like after لَمْ)

steps.m: to guide or show the way

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to guide' into the noun 'guidance'
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'
Added ال to make it specific guidance. This word is what is being followed in the phrase 'if we follow the guidance'
This is a preposition showing accompaniment

steps.m: with you (in the phrase 'if we follow the guidance with you')
steps.n: Added the pronoun ك (you) to show who is being accompanied. This word is part of a conditional phrase explaining what might happen if they follow the guidance alongside Muhammad.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Doubling the middle letter shows the action happens multiple times
Adding تَ at the beginning shows the action is happening to someone/something
Adding نُ shows 'we' are receiving the action, and the sukun at the end shows it's connected to what comes after
This is a basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects to أَرْضِنَآ (our land) to show they're talking about being taken from their land.
Added a sukun (ْ) on the ن to show where the word stops. This helps with pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning.
Added marks (fatha and sukun) to the root letters to make the basic word for 'earth'
Adding kasra at the end shows this word is being possessed or owned by something
Added نَآ at the end to show the land belongs to "us". The آ is a lengthened ا that comes at the end of words. This word follows مِنْ (from) in the ayah.
This particle turns a statement into a question, showing surprise or drawing attention to what follows
Added وَ to connect this question with what came before it
Added لَمْ which makes the question about something that didn't happen in the past
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form by adding shadda, and changed the meaning to 'establish firmly'
Added نُ at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action
This is a preposition showing direction, purpose, or benefit
Adding shadda emphasizes the meaning
Adding هُمْ shows who the preposition is directed to - in this case, 'them' (masculine plural)

steps.m: to make something protected or sacred

steps.n: Adding the vowels changes this from a concept into a specific place that is protected

steps.m: a sanctuary (object of نُمَكِّن - we establish)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this is the object being established in the verse - Allah is establishing this as a sanctuary for them
Changes from verb to noun meaning 'one who does the action'
Added ًا to show this word describes حَرَمًا (sanctuary) in the ayah
The word stays the same - it's describing something as safe/secure, with a special ending (-an) showing it's being used as a description
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: it is being gathered (fruits are gathered to it)
steps.n: Changes to passive voice present tense, showing that something is happening to the subject rather than the subject doing the action. In this ayah, it's talking about fruits being gathered to the sacred sanctuary.
The alif maqsurah (ى with two dots) at the end shows this is a complete passive verb, indicating something is being gathered/collected
This is a preposition showing direction or destination
The ى changes to ي before adding pronouns
Added pronoun هِ meaning 'him/it' referring back to the sanctuary (حَرَمًا) mentioned earlier
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic noun meaning the result of producing fruit
Added the feminine plural ending to show multiple fruits
Added the ُ ending to show this word is the subject doing the action in the sentence. These fruits are what is being brought to the sacred sanctuary.
The doubled ل with damma makes it mean 'every' or 'all' without exception
Adding kasra shows this word connects to ثَمَرٰتُ (fruits) in the ayah, showing these are all types of fruits

steps.m: to be a thing, to exist

steps.n: Root letters combined with fatha and sukun to make the common noun 'thing'
The hamza at the end changes to hamza on the line when it comes at the end of a word
The ي is written as ى when followed by hamza
Added tanwin kasra to show this word belongs to كُلِّ (every/all)

steps.m: to provide with what is needed to live

steps.n: Changed the word from an action (providing) to a thing (provision) by adding the special vowel pattern

steps.m: a provision (object being gathered in يُجْبَىٰٓ)
steps.n: Added ًا (tanween) to show this is a direct object - it's the provision being gathered. Also has a shadda on ر
Basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from

Added shadda (ّ) on م because it comes after another word ending in ن. This is just for easier pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning. In this ayah, it shows something coming from لَّدُنَّا (Our presence).
Becomes a noun meaning presence or nearness
Adds vowels to show this is a noun meaning 'presence' or 'nearness'
Added shadda for emphasis
Added نَا to show this is from Allah (referring to Himself as 'Us')
The ل at the start got stronger/doubled to emphasize that this is coming directly from Allah
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this statement to the previous one. This shows that what follows is connected to but different from what came before.
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from regular verb 'became many' to special form meaning 'more/most numerous'

steps.m: most of them (subject of لَا يَعْلَمُونَ)
steps.n: The word 'most' (أَكْثَر) got 'of them' (هُمْ) attached to it, making it 'most of them'
This is a negative particle that makes the following verb يَعْلَمُونَ (they know) into its opposite meaning (they do not know). It's one of the most common words used to say 'no' or 'not' in Arabic.
Added fatha mark (َ) on the ل to show how it's pronounced with an 'a' sound
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changes from past tense (he knew) to present tense (he knows) by adding يَ at the start

steps.m: they know (referring to those who don't follow guidance)
steps.n: Changed from 'he knows' to 'they know' by adding ون at the end to show multiple people
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation of this question word that asks about quantity

steps.m: And how many (introduces a rhetorical question about أَهْلَكْنَا - how many We destroyed)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before. This combination often introduces examples in the Quran, asking 'and how many?' to emphasize that something happened many times.
Added vowels to connect the letters into a past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning makes the action causative - someone making the destruction happen to something else
Adding نَا at the end changes 'he destroyed' to 'we destroyed'

steps.m: from (connects قَرْيَةٍۭ to what comes before)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows something comes from or is part of something else. In this ayah, it connects 'town/city' (قَرْيَةٍۭ) to the previous part of the sentence.
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters

steps.m: a village/town (a place where people settle)
steps.n: Adds -ah ending to make a noun meaning 'a place where people live together'
The word gets a special ending mark (tanween kasra) to show it's receiving the action of being destroyed - it's what was destroyed in this ayah
Added short vowels (fat-ha and kasra) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he became arrogant'

steps.m: she became arrogant with wealth (subject of مَعِيشَتَهَا)
steps.n: Added ت at the end to show 'she' did the action. In this ayah, it refers to مَعِيشَتَهَا (their livelihood) which is feminine

steps.m: to live, exist, or make a living

steps.n: Added مَ at the beginning to transform this into a noun showing how or where something happens
Added ة to make it a specific instance or concrete example of living

steps.m: her way of life (object of بَطِرَتْ/exulted in)
steps.n: Added هَا to show this belongs to her/it (feminine). This word is what was exulted in or boasted about (object of بَطِرَتْ)

steps.m: those (referring to the dwellings mentioned before)
steps.n: This is a demonstrative pronoun that points to something far away (in this case, the dwellings of destroyed towns mentioned earlier)

steps.m: so those (referring to the dwellings mentioned before)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what was mentioned before (the destruction of towns)

steps.m: to settle, dwell, or be at rest

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added مَ at beginning and sukun on middle letter to create a noun meaning 'place where the action happens'
Changes singular 'dwelling' into plural 'dwellings' by adding long a sound
Changed to مَسٰكِنُ with a damma ending to show these dwellings are doing the action in the sentence
Added هُمْ at the end to show these are their dwellings
This is a negative particle that changes the meaning of the verb after it (تُسْكَن) from 'is lived in' to 'was not lived in'. It refers to something that didn't happen in the past.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: it is dwelt in (describing مَسٰكِنُهُمْ - their dwellings)
steps.n: Changed to passive voice present tense. This word describes their dwellings (مَسٰكِنُهُمْ) in the ayah, showing they are not inhabited.
This is a basic particle that shows where something starts or comes from
The doubled م (shown by ّ) adds emphasis to show importance. The small ن at the end (ۢ) helps connect it smoothly to the next word بَعْدِهِمْ (after them).
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create the basic noun form
The word 'after' gets a special ending (-i) because it's connected to the next word

steps.m: after them (referring to the people of the destroyed towns)
steps.n: Adding هِم shows this refers to 'them' - specifically the people of the towns mentioned earlier in the verse who were destroyed
This is a basic particle used to show exceptions, like saying 'but' or 'except' in English. It has no root because it's a helper word.
Added shadda and alif to complete the word. In this ayah, it shows that قَلِيلًا (a little/few) is an exception to what came before.

steps.m: to be small in amount or number

steps.n: Added ي between the second and third letters and vowel marks to make it into a word describing something that has this quality

steps.m: a little while (describing how long the houses remained inhabited)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this word describes how long something happened. In this ayah, it tells us the houses were only inhabited for a little while after their owners were destroyed.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed first vowel to damma, dropped middle weak letter و, added shadda, and added نا to show 'we' in past tense
Added conjunction و with fatha to connect this word to what came before it
This is the basic pronoun meaning 'we'. It's used for emphasis in this ayah.
Added damma (ُ) at the end to show this is the subject (doer) of the verb كُنَّا (we were) in this ayah

steps.m: to inherit or receive something from someone else

steps.n: Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'inherited'
Added alif after first letter to show who does the action
Added ين to show multiple inheritors
Added ال to make it specific and added final َ because it's the predicate of كُنَّا (we were)
This is a basic particle that can mean either 'what' (for questions) or 'not' (for negation). Here it's used with كَانَ to say 'was not'

steps.m: and not (negates كَانَ in this ayah)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this idea with what came before it. The و here means 'and', connecting this statement to the previous one.
Added fatha marks to show past tense and make it mean 'he was'
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic that doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to care for, nurture, develop to completion
master, one who nurtures to completion
steps.n: The root means 'to nurture', adding the double ب makes it mean 'the one who nurtures' - like changing 'teach' to 'teacher'
Added ُ to show this is the doer of the verb كَانَ (was) in this ayah
Added كَ to show this master belongs to you (the one being spoken to). This word is doing the action of كَانَ (was) in the ayah.
Added vowels to connect the letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed to active participle form showing who does the action. This word is destroying 'the towns' (الْقُرَىٰ) in this ayah.

steps.m: to settle, dwell, or gather in a place

steps.n: Changes from root meaning to plural noun form

steps.m: the villages, the towns (object of مُهْلِكِى - destroyers of)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to specify these particular villages. This word is being acted upon by مُهْلِكِى (destroyers of) in this ayah.
Added ى to complete the proper noun form. The ى here is an alif maqsurah which is a special way to write alif at the end of words.
Basic form of the preposition that shows a limit or endpoint
Added vowel mark and shadda to show correct pronunciation
Added alif maqsura at the end to complete the word. This word connects to يَبْعَثَ (he sends) to show the endpoint of the action.

steps.m: to send forth or raise up

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fathas to make the basic past tense verb
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense, with sukun on ب to help pronunciation, and damma at end for present tense pattern
Changed the ending vowel to fatha to show it's in the subjunctive mood, often used after certain words like أَن (that)

steps.m: in (location within أُمِّهَا - its mother city)
steps.n: Basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it shows the location where the messenger is sent.

steps.m: in (location within أُمِّهَا - its mother city)
steps.n: The ي is written as ى with a small alif (ٓ) above it when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to be in front, to lead as an example

steps.n: Root letters combined with middle letter doubled to make the noun 'mother'
Shows this word belongs to or is connected to something else (in this case, القرى - the towns)

steps.m: its main city (referring to القرى - the towns)
steps.n: Adds feminine possessive pronoun هَا referring back to القرى (the towns). In this context, أُمّ means 'main city' rather than literal 'mother'.
The و makes it mean someone who does this action as their main role
Added ًا to show this is receiving the action of sending in the verse
Added vowels to connect the letters. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end.
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, putting sukun on ت, changing middle vowel to ُ, and returning و at end
The ى changes to ي before attachments
Adds هِمْ to show this applies to 'them' (the people). This word connects to يَتْلُوا۟ (recites) in the ayah, showing what is being recited upon them.

steps.m: to show, make clear, be evident

steps.n: The fatha vowel helps form the singular noun 'sign/verse'
Regular feminine plural formed by adding ات to singular noun. This word is the direct object (what is being recited) of the verb يَتْلُوا (to recite).
Adds possession - 'our signs/verses'. This word is the direct object (what is being recited) of the verb يَتْلُوا۟ which means 'he recites'.
مَا is a negative particle that negates the verb كُنَّا (we were) in this context
Added وَ (and) at the start to connect with what came before
Added fathas to make past tense. The middle و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Changed from past tense 'was' to a command 'be!' by dropping the middle letter and changing vowels. Like telling someone 'Be quiet!'
Added shadda to emphasize the command
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did this action in the past
Added مُ at start and ِ in middle to show who does the destroying. This word is connected to كُنَّا (we were) in this ayah.
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it
Added ي at the end to show this belongs to me ('my')
Added ة to make it refer to one specific place of settling
Changed from single village to plural villages by changing the middle vowels and ending
Added ٱلْ to make it specific

steps.m: the villages, the towns (object of مُهْلِكَ - destroyer of)
steps.n: Added alif khanjariyah (ٰ) to show long vowel sound, with small alif (ٓ) for pause
This is a complete word that means 'except'. It connects to the previous statement to show an exception. In this ayah, it connects to مُهْلِكِى (destroyers) to show who was excluded from destruction.

steps.m: to inhabit, settle, be familiar with

steps.n: Connected the root letters together with fatha and sukun to form the basic noun meaning 'inhabitants' or 'people'. This word is the subject (doer) of ظٰلِمُونَ (wrongdoers) in this ayah.
The damma (u sound) at the end shows this word is doing the action - it's the people who are being wrongdoers

steps.m: its inhabitants (referring to الْقُرَىٰ - the towns)
steps.n: Added هَا which refers back to الْقُرَىٰ (the towns) mentioned earlier in the ayah. This shows whose inhabitants are being discussed.
The و connects this phrase to the previous clause in the verse
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changes from a past-tense verb (he wronged) to describe the person doing the action (wrongdoer)

steps.m: those who act unjustly (referring to multiple people)
steps.n: Adding ون at the end makes this refer to three or more males who do the action
This is the base interrogative/negative particle that can mean either 'what' (asking a question) or 'not' (making something negative)
Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement, like saying 'and' in English
Changed the final ا to آ - this is just a writing variation that doesn't change the meaning
Changed to passive voice to show receiving rather than giving
Added ت at the end to show 'you' were the one who received something in the past
Added ُ to show this is about 'you' (one person)
Added م to show this is about multiple people being given something. This word connects to شَىْءٍ (anything) in the ayah, showing what was given.
This is a basic particle word that shows something comes from somewhere else or belongs to something else
The shadda (doubled م) makes the word stronger to emphasize the connection between things (in this ayah, it connects 'what you are given' to 'things')

steps.m: to want or wish for something

steps.n: Root letters ش-ي-أ form the simple noun شَيْء meaning 'thing'. Like how we turn 'want' into something you can touch or own. Here it means 'anything' that was given, and has an 'in' sound at the end because it follows the word 'from'.
The hamza (ء) at the end causes the middle letter ي to change to ى (alif maqsura). This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Added the tanwin kasra (ٍ) at the end to show this is any general thing, not a specific thing. This matches how it's used after مِن (from/of) in the phrase 'from any thing'.
Changed verb into noun showing the result of enjoying/benefiting
Added damma to show this word is the subject or after 'is'
The alif (ا) was written as a small line above the letter (ٰ) - both spellings mean the same thing
Added فَ at beginning to connect with previous statement, showing this is a result or consequence
Added و to make it into a noun about living. The last ي changes to و because it's easier to say.
Added ة to make it a feminine noun meaning 'life'
Added ٱلْ to specify we're talking about a particular kind of life (in this case, worldly life)

steps.m: of the life (describing الدنيا - this worldly life)
steps.n: Added a kasra ( ِ ) at the end to show this word is connected to الدنيا, making it mean 'the worldly life'
Changed the word to mean 'lower' or 'nearer' by adding special vowel marks and changing و to ي. This makes it feminine, like saying 'the closer one' for a feminine thing.
Added ٱلْ at the beginning to make it specific, like saying 'the lower life' instead of just any lower thing. This word is describing الْحَيَوٰةِ (life) in this ayah.
Added a shadda (doubling mark) on the د to make the meaning stronger
Added kasra (ِ) under first letter to change from action to thing
Added ة at end to make it a feminine noun meaning 'decoration'
Added 'wa' (وَ) meaning 'and' at start, plus 'u' sound at end (ُ) to show this word is a subject working with متٰع (enjoyment)
Removes وَ (and) from start and adds ها (her/its) at end to show this decoration belongs to something mentioned before
Combines 'and' with the relative pronoun 'what'
This word is used to show location or possession, like saying something is 'at' or 'with' someone
Added vowels to make it a noun meaning 'presence' or 'nearness'
Added fatha at the end to make it a preposition. In this ayah, it connects with اللَّهِ to mean 'with Allah' or 'in Allah's presence'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Changed from 'a god' to 'The God' by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows emphasis.
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and changed initial alif to wasl alif since it connects with previous word
Connected the letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to make it a noun describing a quality
Added double dammah to show this is an indefinite noun in the nominative case
Added أ at the beginning and changed the vowels to make it mean 'more' of the quality
Added و at the beginning to connect it with the previous word خَيْرٌ (better)
The alif maksura (ى) at the end shows this is a comparative word meaning 'more X' or 'most X'
Starting with the question particle أَ which turns a statement into a question
Added فَ which shows this question follows from what was mentioned before

steps.m: then will you not? (connects to تَعْقِلُونَ - use your reason)
steps.n: Added لا which makes the question negative. This combination أَفَلَا is asking why someone is not doing something that should be obvious from the previous statement. Here it's asking 'then will you not use your reason?' (تَعْقِلُونَ)
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding تَ at start and changing vowel pattern. This shows the action is happening now.

steps.m: about what (object of يُشْرِكُونَ - they associate)

steps.m: about what (object of يُشْرِكُونَ - they associate)
steps.n: The ن and م combine together with a shadda (ّ) to make it easier to say. This is like how in English 'in making' often sounds like 'im-making'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Vowels changed from 'a-a-a' to 'no vowel-i-u' to make it present tense 'he associates'
Added يُ at beginning to make it present tense
Added ون at the end to show multiple people doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to those who associate partners with Allah.
The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures and takes care of others completely

steps.m: lord (as the one doing something in يَعْلَمُ)
steps.n: The ُ shows this word is doing the action of knowing (يَعْلَمُ) in this ayah

steps.m: your lord (as the one doing something in يَعْلَمُ)
steps.n: The كَ at the end means 'your' (when talking to one male). This word is the subject (doer) of the verb يَعْلَمُ (knows/is knowing).
Adding conjunction prefix وَ meaning 'and'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Added يَ at start to show present tense, sukun on ع to help pronunciation, and ُ at end to show 'he' is doing it. This verb tells what your Lord (رَبُّكَ) knows.
This is a special word that connects two parts of a sentence. Here it shows what Allah knows - the things that are concealed.
Basic past tense verb form with fatha vowels
Final damma shows normal present tense

steps.m: chest, breast (the front part that contains the heart)

steps.n: Connected the root letters together to form the basic noun for a single chest
Changed the vowel pattern and added و to make it plural, showing there are many chests

steps.m: their chests (what they conceal in their hearts)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show these are their chests. In this ayah, it refers to what people hide in their hearts, as shown by the verb تُكِنُّ (conceal) before it.

steps.m: what (refers to what they reveal in يُعْلِنُونَ)
steps.n: This is a special word that refers to things. Here it specifically refers to what people make known or reveal, connecting to the word يُعْلِنُونَ (they reveal) that comes after it.

steps.m: and what (refers to what they reveal in يُعْلِنُونَ)
steps.n: The وَ at the start connects this to the previous statement about what their hearts conceal (تُكِنُّ صُدُورُهُمْ), showing Allah knows both their secrets and what they reveal
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Added يُ at start and changed vowels to show present tense
Added ونَ to show multiple people doing the action. This word follows وَمَا (and what) in the ayah.
This is a personal pronoun that means 'he'
Added diacritics to show proper pronunciation
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'

This is the unique name of God in Islam, derived from إِلَه but with special changes to show it refers to the One True God only. The ال is merged with the first letter, and the middle letter has a shadda to make it stronger.
This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it. It is not derived from a root as it is a primitive particle.
Added fatha to show how the word is pronounced
The alif is elongated to add emphasis to the negation, making it stronger and absolute

steps.m: to worship or be devoted to

steps.n: Changed from a root meaning 'to worship' into a noun meaning 'a god' by adding the vowel marks. Like changing 'worship' into 'the one who is worshipped'
The small alif above makes the 'aa' sound longer when reading
Added َ (fatha) at the end because لا (no/not) requires this ending. This is part of the phrase 'لا إله' meaning 'there is no god'
This is a basic word meaning 'except'. It's used to exclude something from what was mentioned before it.
Basic form of the third person masculine singular pronoun
Added damma (ُ) on ه and fatha (َ) on و to show this is the subject pronoun. In this ayah, it refers back to Allah mentioned right after it.
Added fatha to show this is a preposition meaning 'for' or 'to'

Added the U-vowel at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence
Added 'the' at the beginning to show we're talking about a specific praise

Changed the ي to ى which is just a different way of writing the same sound at the end of words
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this is about being first
Added the feminine ending ىٰ to make it refer to feminine things

steps.m: to come after, to be last

Added ال to make it definite, referring to the specific hereafter
Added و (and) at start and ِ at end to show it's possessed by له (to Him/His) in the phrase له الحمد
Added pronoun 'him' to show who something is for
Added conjunction 'and' to connect this phrase to what came before it in the verse (لَهُ ٱلْحَمْدُ - to Him is praise)
This is the basic concept of judging or ruling

Added the definite article 'al' at the beginning and damma at the end to show this is the subject belonging to له (to Him)
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation

Added هِ at the end to show the direction is towards 'him'

steps.m: and towards him/and to him (connects to تُرْجَعُونَ)

Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to make it passive, showing the action is being done to someone rather than by someone

Added ون at the end to show this applies to a group of people
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form. The middle letter و drops out because it's between two other consonants. This is common in Arabic with weak letters like و.
Added vowels (fatha) to make it 'I saw' in the past tense

steps.m: have you all seen/considered? (introducing إِن جَعَلَ)
steps.n: Added أَ at the start to make it a question, changed to 'you all' (plural) form instead of 'I', and lengthened the middle letter. This question asks people to think about something.
This is a conditional particle that introduces a hypothetical situation. In this ayah, it starts a condition about 'if Allah made the night continuous', connecting to the verb جَعَلَ (made/set).

steps.m: to make something into something else, to put, to set

Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns

This is a unique word that refers specifically to the one true God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the shadda emphasizes the ل, and the ُ shows this is the subject of جَعَلَ (made/placed) in this ayah.
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
The ى changes to يْ when adding a suffix - this is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning
Added كُمُ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to

Adds vowel marks to create noun meaning 'perpetual'

steps.m: continuously, permanently (describes how Allah makes the night in this ayah)
steps.n: Adding ًا at the end changes this from a descriptive word to showing how something is done. In this ayah, it describes how Allah makes the night continuous.
Basic preposition showing direction or movement toward something
Added an alif symbol (ىٰ) at the end to show this is connecting to the next word يَوْمِ (Day). In this ayah, it's showing the extent of time - 'until the Day of Resurrection'
Connected the root letters together with vowel marks to form the basic noun 'day'
Added kasra at the end to show this word means 'of' something
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changes verb 'stood' into noun meaning 'act of standing'
Added ة to make it refer to a specific instance - in this case, the Day of Resurrection
Added the definite article to specify this is THE resurrection
Added kasra at the end because this word is possessed by يَوْمِ (day) in the phrase 'Day of Resurrection'
This is a question word used to ask 'who?' about people. Here it's asking which deity besides Allah could bring light, connecting to إِلٰهٌ (god/deity) that comes after it.

steps.m: to worship or be devoted to

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to worship' into noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
The small alif above makes the 'aa' sound longer when reading
Added double-damma (ٌ) at the end to show this is talking about any god, not a specific one

steps.m: to change or be different from something

steps.m: other than (describes إلٰه/god in the nominative case)

Added shadda to show emphasis on the ل

Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

Changed the root letters into past tense 'he saw' by adding the vowel marks
Added أَ at the beginning to make it a question
Added تَ at the end to make it 'you' (masculine singular)

steps.m: to make or set something in a certain way

Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns

This is a preposition showing something is positioned above or affecting something else. It's one of the basic prepositions in Arabic that doesn't come from a root.

Added ال to specify we're talking about the daytime in particular. The ن gets a shadda because it comes after ال.

steps.m: to worship or be devoted to

steps.m: you (all) see (in response to أَفَلَا 'then do you not')

Added 'ت' and kasra at the end to show this mercy belongs to someone (possession)

Added the pronoun كم with damma marks to show it's for multiple people. This word shows who receives the action of جَعَلَ (He made).

Added 'the' at the beginning and a mark at the end to show this is what was made in the phrase 'He made for you the night'

Added ت at start to make it 'you' (present tense)

Added kasra (ِ) under م to show how to pronounce it correctly

Added kasra (i sound) at the end because it's being possessed by something - shows ownership

The تَ prefix shows it's 'you' and the middle vowel change shows it's happening now
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action (in this case, addressing multiple people)
Connected the letters with vowel marks to make it a usable word
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb يُنَادِيهِمْ (he calls them)
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Changed from basic root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding fathas. The ى at the end shows it's a special verb that ends in ي.
Added يُ at the beginning to show present tense, and changed middle vowel to kasra
Added هِمْ at the end to show who is being called. This verb comes before فَيَقُولُ (then he says) in the ayah.

Adding فَ shows this happens right after the previous action (when Allah calls them)
This is a question word asking about location. It is not derived from a root since it is a primitive particle.

steps.n: Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation. This word is asking about the location of Allah's supposed partners that the disbelievers claimed existed.

steps.m: to partner with or share something

Added kasra to show this word is connected to أُمَّةٍ (nation) that comes after it

steps.m: to witness or testify about what one sees

Adding نَا changes it from a command to past tense and shows we (multiple people) did the action
Adding فَ shows this happened as a result of what came before it in the ayah (We took from every nation a witness)

steps.m: to bring forth or present something
steps.n: This is an unusual root that's mainly used in command form
Added fatha marks to make it mean 'bring!' - like telling someone to bring something
Added وا at the end to show this command is for a group
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun

steps.m: clear proof or evidence (object of هَاتُوا - bring forth)
steps.n: Added alif to strengthen the meaning. This word is what is being commanded to be brought forth by هَاتُوا (bring forth).

steps.m: clear proof or evidence (prepared for possession)
steps.n: Changed the regular alif to a special alif (alif maksura) to show it can be owned by someone
Added second person plural possessive pronoun to show it belongs to multiple people being addressed
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: so they knew (after what was mentioned before)
steps.n: Adds فَ to create a causative/sequential meaning
This is the basic form of the particle that introduces a new statement

steps.m: that, indeed (introduces the fact that الْحَقَّ لِلَّهِ - the truth belongs to Allah)
steps.n: Adding shadda (doubling mark) on ن makes the meaning stronger and more emphatic. This particle introduces the important fact that follows it in the ayah.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a past tense verb
