Connected the root letters that mean similarity or comparison
Changed from verb to noun by adding specific vowel marks
Basic interrogative particle meaning 'what'. In this ayah, it asks about what was given to Qarun. This word is the object of أُوتِىَ (was given).
The alif is written with a special elongation mark (آ) to show it's pronounced longer, but this doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed the vowels to show that something was done to someone rather than someone doing something

steps.m: was given (referring to what Qarun was given)
steps.n: Added و after the first letter because it's easier to pronounce. This is a common change with this type of word pattern.
This is the base form of the proper name Qarun, who was a wealthy person mentioned in the story of Prophet Musa (Moses)
The alif is written as a small alif (ٰ) above the ر instead of a full alif letter. This is just a writing variation and doesn't change the meaning.
The damma (ُ) at the end shows this is the subject of the verb أُوتِىَ (was given) in this verse. People are saying they wish they had what was given to Qarun.
Adding shadda changes this from 'if' to 'indeed'. This emphasizes what comes after it.
Added هُ (he/him) to show who is being talked about. This connects to the next word لَذُو (surely has/possesses).
Added ۥ mark to show the long vowel sound. This doesn't change the meaning, it just helps with pronunciation.
Basic word meaning 'possessor of' or 'owner of'. This is used to connect ownership or qualities.
Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, showing this is definitely or surely true. This emphasizes that Qarun really did have great fortune.
Connected the root letters and added fatha on first letter with shadda on last letter to make it a noun
Added two dots under (ٍ) to show this is something owned/possessed, like saying 'of a portion' instead of just 'a portion'
The ي between the second and third root letters makes this an adjective showing something has a strong quality of being magnificent
The ٍ (tanween kasra) at the end shows this is describing something general (not specific) and is connected to the word حَظٍّ (fortune)
Added fatha vowels to make it past tense
Changed to common past tense form with alif
Added و at the beginning to mean 'and'. This word is connected to يُلَقَّىٰهَآ (receives it) in this ayah.
Starting with the basic relative pronoun and adding ي to make it a complete word
Adding ن at the end makes it refer to multiple people instead of just one
Adding ال at the beginning makes it mean 'those who' instead of just 'who'. This word refers to the people who were given knowledge in this verse.
Changed to passive form to show receiving something rather than bringing it
Added و because this is a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter)
Added وا to show multiple people received something. This word connects to العِلْمَ (knowledge) in the ayah, showing what they were given.
Changed the verb into a noun by adding kasra on first letter and sukun on middle letter

steps.m: the knowledge (object of أُوتُوا - were given)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite (the knowledge) and fatha at the end because it's the object of the verb أُوتُوا (were given)
This is a word expressing strong warning or threat, like saying 'terrible destruction!' in English
Added fatha to prepare for connection with next part

steps.m: woe to you all (said by الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْعِلْمَ - those given knowledge)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this warning is directed at multiple people. In this ayah, those who were given knowledge are warning others about their behavior.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed to a noun meaning the result of returning/giving back. This word is described as 'better' (خَيْرٌ) in this ayah.
Added ال to specify this is THE one true deity, and the hamza was dropped because this is a special word used so often
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word ثَوَابُ (reward) before it. The ل has a shadda to emphasize it. The alif at the start changed to a special connecting alif (ٱ).
Changes the word into a noun meaning 'goodness' or 'that which is good'
Adds double-dammah to show this is an indefinite noun in the nominative case
Basic interrogative/relative pronoun meaning 'who' or 'whoever'
Added ل at beginning to show purpose or direction
Added shadda on ل to emphasize the meaning. This phrase connects to خَيْرٌ (better) in the ayah, showing who Allah's reward is better for.
Connected the root letters that mean safety or security
Added vowel marks to make it a past tense verb about being safe
Changed the word to Form IV by adding an alif after the first letter, which changes the meaning from 'being safe' to 'having faith/believing' because faith gives safety

steps.m: to do, to act, to work

steps.n: Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb
Added و at the beginning to connect this action with what came before in the verse
Added vowel marks to show this is a past tense verb

steps.m: to make something good or right

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and kasra under second letter to show who does the action
Added tanween alif to show this describes how the action عَمِلَ (he worked/did) was done - he did it righteously
Basic negative particle that negates what comes after it

steps.m: and not (connects to ثَوَابُ ٱللَّهِ خَيْرٌ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to the previous statement about Allah's reward being better
The root 'meet' becomes 'is being received' by adding yu- at start and doubling the middle letter
Added alif maqsurah at the end due to weak letter rule

steps.m: is made to receive it (referring to the reward mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added feminine object pronoun referring back to the reward (ثَوَابُ) mentioned in the ayah
This is a basic word that shows something is left out from what was mentioned before. In this ayah, it shows that only the patient ones receive what was mentioned.
Added the vowel mark ِ (kasra) under the إ and doubled the ل with ّ (shadda) to show how it's pronounced.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changes the verb into a noun describing someone who has this quality
Adds 'the' to make it specific and doubles the ص because it comes after ال
Adds ُونَ to show this refers to multiple people who are patient

steps.m: to sink or cause to sink into the ground

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Added نَا to show 'we' did the action. This verb acts on الْأَرْضَ (the earth) in this ayah.

steps.m: and so we sank (something) into the ground (acting on الْأَرْضَ)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happened as a consequence of previous events
This is a basic preposition showing how something was done. It connects to the verb فَخَسَفْنَا (we caused to swallow) to show how the action was done.
The هِ suffix refers to Qarun (mentioned earlier in the story). This shows he was the one who was swallowed up.
The small ۦ is just a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning
Added vowel marks and connected letters to form the noun 'house'. The و from the root becomes ا in this form.
Added kasra at the end to show this word will be possessed by something
Added بِ to show this is being used with the meaning 'with' or 'by'
Added هِ to show this belongs to him

steps.m: and with his house (connected to فَخَسَفْنَا)
steps.n: Added وَ to connect this to the previous phrase فَخَسَفْنَا (so We caused to swallow)

steps.m: land, earth, ground (anything you can walk on)

steps.n: Basic noun form of 'earth/land' with marks showing how to say it (fatha-sukun pattern)

steps.m: the earth (object of خَسَفْنَا/We caused to swallow)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it specific and َ at the end to show it's receiving the action of swallowing in the verse
This is a negative particle that negates the verb كَانَ (was) that comes after it in this ayah
The فَ shows this negation is happening as a result of Allah causing the earth to swallow him and his house (mentioned in the previous part of the ayah)
Added fathas to make it past tense. This is the theoretical form before the weak letter change.

steps.m: he was (subject of يَنصُرُونَ - they help/support)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic to make words easier to say.
This is a preposition that shows direction or purpose. Here it connects to the verb يَنصُرُونَ (they help) to show who is being helped.

steps.m: for him (referring to قارون, the one being helped)
steps.n: The هُ refers to Qarun (قارون) who is mentioned earlier in the story. The ۥ mark is just to show the vowel sound is long.
This is a basic preposition showing source, origin, or connection. In this ayah, it connects the word فِئَةٍ (group) to دُونِ (besides/other than), showing who could help him.
Root meaning 'unite' becomes a noun for 'group' by adding vowels
The word gets a special ending (-in) to show it's part of a phrase meaning 'any group' - like saying 'no group could help him'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel to ُ
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: they help/support him (referring to Qarun in كَانَ)
steps.n: Added هُ (him) to show who receives the help. This describes what Qarun's people couldn't do for him, as mentioned in كَانَ (was) in this ayah.

steps.m: any (when used before an indefinite noun like فِئَةٍ)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from

steps.m: to be below or less than something else

steps.n: Adding damma (ُ) changes it to mean 'below/under'
Added kasra (ِ) at the end because it's connected to 'Allah' - showing we're talking about 'besides Allah'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Changed from 'a god' to 'The God' by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows emphasis.
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word دُونِ (besides/other than) in the ayah
This is a particle that can mean either 'not' or 'what' depending on context. Here it means 'not' because it's negating كَانَ (was)
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This و means 'and'
Added fathas to make past tense verb meaning 'he was'

steps.m: he was (part of negative phrase with مَا)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter). This word is part of a negative phrase with مَا meaning 'he was not'.
This is the basic form of the word that means 'from'
Added fatha (َ) at the end to connect smoothly to the next word المُنتَصِرِينَ (those who help themselves). This is called a 'helping vowel' that makes the words flow together better when speaking.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
Added مُ and ت to show someone doing something for themselves. The ن is added before ت for easier pronunciation.
Added ين to show this refers to multiple people
Added ٱلْ to make it specific

steps.m: the ones who help/defend themselves (object of كَانَ)
steps.n: Added َ at the end to show this word is being acted upon by كَانَ (was)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added أ (alif) at start changes meaning from 'morning' to 'became' - a common pattern in Arabic verbs

steps.m: and he became (subject of يَقُولُونَ - they say)
steps.n: Added 'wa' (and) at the beginning to connect it to what came before
Starting with the basic demonstrative word 'this' and changing it to mean 'who/which'
Added ين to make it refer to multiple people or things
Added ال to make it specific. This word is the subject of يَقُولُونَ (they say) in this ayah.
Added vowel marks and changed ال to ٱ for proper Quranic spelling
Adds emphasis/intensity through doubling of middle root letter

steps.m: to wish something for oneself intensely
steps.n: Added تَ at start to show doing the action to/for yourself - like wishing something for yourself

steps.m: they wished something for themselves intensely
steps.n: Added وْا at the end to show that a group of people (they) did the action

steps.m: to be in a place or state

steps.n: Added fatha vowels to make basic past tense verb
Added مَ at beginning and ا after first root letter to create a noun meaning 'place where something is'

steps.m: his place (object of تَمَنَّوْا - they wished for)
steps.n: Added هُۥ at the end to show possession ('his'). This word is what they wished for in the phrase 'those who had wished for his position'
This is a time word that doesn't come from a root
Adding ٱلْ makes it definite
The بِ prefix means 'in' or 'during'
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ prefix and changing middle vowel
Added ونَ to show multiple people are doing the action
This is an exclamation word expressing surprise or regret
Added ك to strengthen the exclamation
Added أَنَّ to turn 'woe!' into 'woe, it seems that...' - makes it express surprise or regret about something specific
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows emphasis.

steps.m: Allah (as the object of مَنَّ - bestowed favor)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is receiving the action of bestowing favor in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ at end. Shows what is happening now instead of what already happened. Connected to 'they say' (يَقُولُونَ) in the ayah

steps.m: to provide with what is needed

steps.n: Changes the verb (to provide) into a noun (provision/sustenance) - like changing 'feed' to 'food'
Adding ال (al) at the beginning makes it specific, like changing 'food' to 'the food'
The َ (fatha) at the end shows this word is receiving the action of يَبْسُطُ (extends/spreads) - Allah extends the provision
This is a combination of the preposition لِ (for) and مَن (who/whoever)
The root letters ش-ي-أ changed to شَاءَ by adding vowels and turning ي into ا (alif) to make 'he wanted'

steps.m: he wants/wishes (subject of يَبْسُطُ - he extends)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ at end. This word is connected to يَبْسُطُ (he extends) in the ayah, showing Allah extends provision to whom He wills.
Changed the alif to a long alif (آ) for proper writing, but doesn't change the meaning
This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or shows possession. Here it connects 'His servants' (عِبَادِهِۦ) to the previous phrase about Allah extending provision.
Changes from singular 'servant' to plural form meaning 'servants'
The هِۦ suffix shows possession by Allah (His). The ِ (kasra) before it shows this word follows مِنْ (from) in the sentence.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb

steps.m: he measures/controls (connected to يَبْسُطُ - he extends)
steps.n: This word pairs with يَبْسُطُ (he extends) in the ayah to show Allah's complete control over provision - both expanding and restricting it
The و connects this to يَبْسُطُ showing Allah's complete power over provision - both expanding and restricting it
This is a conditional particle that introduces hypothetical situations

steps.m: if not, had it not been
steps.n: Adding لَا (not) changes the meaning to express a negative condition

steps.m: if not, had it not been
steps.n: The ا is written as آ, which is a common spelling variation in the Quran but doesn't change the meaning
This is a particle that connects what people say to the main sentence. It works like quotation marks in English, showing the start of what is being said.
Connected the letters with fatha vowels to show this happened in the past
Added shadda to show the favor was extra special or emphasized

steps.m: he bestowed great favor (subject of لَخَسَفَ)
steps.n: The first letter has a shadda to connect smoothly with the word before it. This word is doing the action in لَخَسَفَ (would have caused to swallow).
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or affecting something else
The ى changes to يْ to connect with the next part. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
Added نَا which means 'us', showing who is being affected by the action in the ayah

steps.m: to sink into the ground or disappear

steps.n: Adding the vowels makes this a past tense verb showing that 'he' did this action

steps.m: he would surely have made (something) sink
steps.n: Adding لَ at the start makes this emphatic - like saying 'surely' or 'definitely'
Basic preposition meaning 'with'

steps.m: with us (connected to لَخَسَفَ - would have swallowed)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show this is happening to 'us'. This word connects to لَخَسَفَ (would have swallowed) to show who would have been swallowed.
This is an exclamation of amazement or regret
Added ك to emphasize the exclamation
Combined وَيْكَ (woe!) with أَنَّ (that/as if) to express amazement or surprise, like saying 'Oh my! It seems that...'

steps.m: woe! it is as if he/it
steps.n: Added هُۥ at the end to show who/what is being referred to
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it negative. Here it changes 'succeed' to 'do not succeed'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start, changing vowels, and adding ُ at end. This verb is negated by لا in the ayah, so it means 'does not succeed'.

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

steps.n: Changes from verb (to reject) to noun (one who rejects)
Adds ون- at the end to make it mean 'multiple people who disbelieve' instead of just one person

steps.m: the ones who reject (truth) (subject of لَا يُفْلِحُ - will not succeed)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it refer to specific disbelievers mentioned in this story. These are the people who won't succeed according to the ayah.
This is the basic form of the feminine demonstrative pronoun meaning 'that'

steps.m: that (feminine, pointing to something far away) (points to ٱلدَّارُ)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show we're pointing to something far away. In this ayah, it's pointing to ٱلدَّارُ (the home/abode).

steps.m: to move in a circle or stay in a place

steps.n: The و (weak letter) becomes ا when we add fatha before it. This pattern creates a noun meaning 'place where something happens' - here it's 'place where people stay'
Added ٱل (al) at the beginning to make it specific, and doubled the د with shadda because it comes after ٱل
Added ُ (damma) at the end to show this is the subject that نَجْعَلُهَا (we make it) acts upon in this ayah
Adding vowels changes it to an adjective meaning 'being in a state of coming after'
Adding ة makes this word feminine, like saying 'the final one' for a feminine noun
Added ال to make it definite. This specific combination (ال + آخِرة) is the standard way to say 'the hereafter'
Added ُ to show this is the subject of the verb نَجْعَلُهَا (we make it)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added ن at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action

steps.m: we make/set it (referring to الدَّارُ ٱلْءَاخِرَةُ - the final home)
steps.n: The ها refers back to الدَّارُ ٱلْءَاخِرَةُ (the final home) mentioned earlier in the ayah
Starting with the basic relative pronoun and adding ال to make it definite. The shadda shows emphasis on the ذ.
Changed from singular to plural by adding ين at the end
Added ل meaning 'for/to' at the beginning. The ل combines with ال, making the ل have a shadda. This shows who will receive the action of نَجْعَلُهَا (we make it).
This is a primitive negative particle that means 'no' or 'not'. No morphological transformation needed as this is a basic particle.
Added vowels to make past tense 'he' form - like turning 'want' into 'wanted'
Added present tense prefix يُ and changed vowels to show ongoing action. The middle و changes to ي due to being between kasra and damma.

steps.m: they want/desire (referring to those who don't seek elevation or corruption)
steps.n: Added masculine plural ending to show multiple people doing the action. This refers back to الَّذِينَ (those who) in the ayah.

steps.m: to rise up, be high, or exalt oneself

steps.n: Forms abstract noun showing the state of being exalted or arrogant. This matches the context of the ayah which talks about those who don't want to show arrogance.

steps.m: arrogantly (in relation to لَا يُرِيدُونَ - they do not want)
steps.n: Changes from a noun to an adverb by adding tanween fatha, showing HOW they don't want to act
This is a basic preposition that shows location or being inside something. In this ayah, it shows location within 'the earth'.
The ي changes to ى at the end of words in the Uthmani script. This is just a writing style change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Added the kasra (ِ) mark under ف to show it's pronounced 'fi'. This helps with correct reading.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make them pronounceable
Adding ٱلْ (al) makes it refer to the specific earth rather than any earth. The ٱ (alif with waslah) shows this connects smoothly with words before it.
The kasra (ِ) shows this word comes after فِى (in/on) in the ayah
Added fatha vowel mark to help with pronunciation
Added the negation word لَا with a fatha. This combines with 'and' to negate what comes after it. Here it's negating عُلُوًّا (superiority/haughtiness) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding ا in the middle turns the verb into a noun meaning the act itself. This word is the object (what is not wanted) in the phrase 'they do not want corruption.'
Adding ًا shows this word is being acted upon - it's what they don't want in the phrase 'they do not want corruption'
Adding vowels turns the root into a usable past tense verb
Added alif after first root letter to create active participle form
Added ة to create a noun meaning the result or end of something
Added ال to make it specific
Added و at beginning to connect to previous phrase
Added a damma (u-sound) at the end to show this is the subject, with a small spelling change in the middle
Added مُ at start and doubled ت between first and second root letters to make it mean 'someone who protects themselves from evil'
Added ين to make it plural, showing it refers to multiple people who guard against evil

steps.m: the ones who guard against evil
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific/definite

steps.m: for the ones who guard against evil
steps.n: Added لِ at the beginning to show this is for/to these people
This is a conditional word that means 'whoever' and introduces the condition 'whoever comes with a good deed.' It connects to the verb جَآءَ (comes/came) that follows it.
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it mean 'he came' in the past tense
Changed to hollow verb pattern by adding alif after ج

steps.m: to do good, to be beautiful

steps.n: Basic adjective form describing the quality of being good/beautiful
The ة transforms the adjective into a feminine noun referring to a single good deed
Adding ال makes it specific, like saying 'the' in English

steps.m: with the good deed (object of جَآءَ - came with)
steps.n: Adding بِ at start means 'with', and ِ at end shows it follows another word. This word follows جَآءَ (came) in the ayah.
Added fatha to show basic meaning of direction or purpose
Added فَ at beginning to show this follows from the previous statement about good deeds
Added هُ at end to show this is 'for him' (referring back to whoever does good deeds)

steps.m: then for him (referring to whoever brings a good deed)
steps.n: Added prolongation mark (ۥ) to indicate long vowel sound on هُ. This is just a pronunciation helper and doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic noun
Added double dammah to make it indefinite and show it's the subject
This is a basic preposition that shows comparison or source
Added هَا to show what is being compared to. The هَا refers back to الحَسَنَة (the good deed) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
The shadda on مِّ appears because this word follows a word ending in ن or م, causing the sounds to merge. This doesn't change the meaning.
This is a special word (relative pronoun) that doesn't come from a root. It refers to any person who does something.
Added و at the beginning to connect this part to the previous statement. This word is the subject (doer) of جَآءَ (came) in this ayah.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Added alif after ج to make it a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter)
Added long alif to emphasize the action of coming
Root س-و-أ changed to سَيِّئ by adding shadda (double letter) to make it mean 'very evil/bad'
Adding ة makes it refer to a specific instance of evil or bad action
Added ال causes doubling (shadda) of س because it's a 'sun letter'
Adds preposition bi meaning 'with'

steps.m: with/by the evil deed/sin (object of جَآءَ)
steps.n: The kasra ending shows this word is affected by the preposition بِ. This whole phrase describes what someone 'came with' (جَآءَ)
Basic connecting particle that shows this follows from the previous statement

steps.m: so not/then not (connects to يُجْزَى - they will be recompensed)
steps.n: Added لَا which negates what comes after it. In this ayah, it's saying 'so they will not be recompensed' connecting to the verb يُجْزَى that follows it.

steps.m: to give what is due (reward or punishment)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: he is given what is due (passive of جَآءَ)
steps.n: Added يُ at the beginning and changed vowels to make it passive, showing someone receiving the action. This means instead of 'giving', it becomes 'being given'.
Added ال at the beginning and doubled the ذ to create the basic relative pronoun. This word points to a specific person or thing that does something.
Added ين at the end to make it refer to multiple people. This word is connected to عَمِلُوا۟ (they did/performed) in the ayah, showing who did the action.
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb
Added وا to show multiple people (they) did the action
Connected the letters and added vowels to make a basic noun
Changes the word to describe something as being evil or bad
Makes it plural to talk about multiple evil deeds
Adds 'the' to talk about specific evil deeds
Shows this word belongs to or follows another word in the sentence

This is a basic particle that shows an exception. In this ayah, it's connecting to يَعْمَلُونَ (they used to do) to show that people who did bad deeds will only be repaid for what they used to do, nothing more.
This is a relative pronoun that connects to the verb يَعْمَلُونَ (they do/used to do) in this ayah. It asks about or refers to the actions that were done.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter in the past tense
Added وا to show multiple people (they) and ُ before it to connect it smoothly. This word is the subject of يَعْمَلُونَ (they do/work) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Adds يَ at the start to change from past to present/future tense

steps.m: they work/act/do (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier in كَانُوا)
steps.n: Makes the verb plural masculine to match the subject (the disbelievers) of كَانُوا (they were)

steps.m: they used to work/act/do (object of كَانُوا)
steps.n: Adds a small 'a' sound (fatha) at the end because it follows the helper verb كَانُوا (they were) - like saying 'they were doing' instead of just 'they do'
Adding the shadda (double letter mark) changes this from a basic 'if/that' particle into a strong emphasis word. It makes what comes after it more certain and important.

steps.m: the one who/that which - relative pronoun that connects a description to what is being described
steps.n: This is a complete demonstrative pronoun that consists of the definite article ال merged with ذي. The shadda indicates the assimilation of ل with ذ.

steps.m: he made required (the Quran upon you)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word is connected to القرءان (the Quran) which is what was made required, and عليك (upon you) showing who it was required for.
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
The ى changes to يْ when adding a suffix
Added ك at the end to show this applies to 'you' (when speaking to one male)
Added short vowels (fatha) to make the past tense verb 'he read'

steps.m: recitation, that which is recited abundantly
steps.n: Changes verb 'read' into noun meaning 'that which is recited a lot' (Quran)
Added the definite article 'the' to specify this particular recited text
Added the fatḥa ending to show this is the object of the verb فَرَضَ (ordained/prescribed)
Added alif after first letter to show who does the action
Added لَ at beginning to add emphasis (connected to إِنَّ at start of verse)
Added nominative case marker to indicate subject status
Added pronoun كَ meaning 'you' to show who is being returned. This connects to the full meaning where Allah is promising to return the Prophet to a place of return.
Basic preposition indicating direction or destination

Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end with a small alif above it. This is just a spelling variation and doesn't change the meaning. This preposition connects to the word مَعَادٍ (place of return) that comes after it.
Root letters connected with a fat-ha (a) and sukoon (stop mark) to make 'returning'
Added مَ at the beginning to show this is a place where the action happens. The و changed to ا because it's a weak letter.

steps.m: place of return (being described in this ayah)
steps.n: Added two dots under the last letter to show it's describing a specific place of return
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he said' to a command 'say!' by dropping last letter and changing middle vowel to 'u'
The middle letter و is dropped because it's a weak letter in the command form. This word appears in the Quran when Allah is commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to say something.
Added fatha and shadda to create noun meaning 'one who nurtures completely'
Added shadda on first letter for emphasis since this word refers to Allah
Added kasra to prepare word for possession marker
Added ى to show this is 'my Lord'
Added elongation mark for proper Quranic recitation at pause
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: more knowing (than those who are misguided)
steps.n: Added hamza at start and changed vowels to make comparative form. In this ayah, it compares Allah's knowledge of who is guided versus who is misguided.
Basic interrogative/relative pronoun used to refer to people. In this ayah, it's asking about who came with guidance.
Connected the root letters ج-ي-أ with the vowel marks َ (fatha) to make the past tense verb 'he came'
Added ا (alif) after ج to make it a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter)
The alif is written with a special mark (آ) to stretch the sound, making it longer and more emphatic
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the act or result of guidance. The ى at the end is because this is a special type of word that ends in ى instead of ي.
Added ال to make it specific guidance
Added بِ to show this guidance is being used as a tool or means. The ٱ is just another way to write ا, and ىٰ is another way to write ى.
This is a question word that asks about people (who?) but can also mean 'whoever' when used in statements. Here it means 'whoever' since it's not asking a question.

steps.m: and whoever (is connected to مَن جَآءَ 'whoever came' earlier in the ayah)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This creates a contrast between two groups - those who came with guidance and those who are in clear error.
This is a basic pronoun meaning 'he'. In this ayah, it's the subject that connects to the phrase 'in clear error' (فِى ضَلٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ). It's like saying 'he is in clear error'.
Basic preposition showing location or state. In this ayah, it shows someone being in a state of clear misguidance.

steps.m: to wander off the right path

steps.n: Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
Changed from a verb (action) to a noun meaning 'straying' - makes it more intense
The word got tanween (two dots under) because it comes after فِى (in), showing it means 'in misguidance'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start to make it mean 'the one who' and changed inside vowels to show it's describing something that makes things clear
Added two dots under at end (tanween) to show it's indefinite - like saying 'a' instead of 'the'
Adding the double letter mark (shadda) makes the meaning stronger
This is a basic particle that can either ask 'what?' or say 'not' depending on context. Here it's saying 'not' about تَرْجُوٓا۟ (you were hoping)
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Command form - shortened by dropping middle letter و and changing vowels to make it a direct order meaning 'be!'
Added تَ at the end to show this is talking about 'you' (masculine) in the past
Added vowels to connect the letters. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end.
Added ت at start to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense. The middle letter gets sukun (ْ) and the last vowel changes to damma (ُ) with و returning.
The ا at the end with marks shows this is now 'you all' instead of just 'you'
This is a connecting word that links the verb تَرْجُوٓا۟ (you were hoping) with what comes after it. It works like 'that' in English when you say 'I hope that...'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form past tense
Changed to passive by adding يُ at start to show someone else is doing the action to it

steps.m: it is given/delivered (to إِلَيْكَ - to you)
steps.n: Added alif maksura with madd to elongate final sound. This word connects to إِلَيْكَ (to you) in the ayah.
This is the basic preposition meaning 'to' or 'towards'. It's one of the most common prepositions in Arabic.
The ى changes to ي with sukun when adding a suffix. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to you (referring to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ in this ayah)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show this means 'to you'. In this ayah, it's talking about the Book (Quran) being sent to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: book (subject of يُلْقَىٰٓ - is sent down)
steps.n: Changed the vowels and added ا to make it mean the result of writing - a book. This word is what's being sent down in the verse.

steps.m: the book (subject of يُلْقَىٰٓ - is sent down)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ at the beginning to mean 'the' and ُ at the end to show it's doing the action in the sentence
This is a basic particle used to exclude something from what was mentioned before. It's like saying 'but not' or 'except for' in English.
Added shadda and alif at the end to complete the word. In this ayah, it introduces an exception - the Book was sent as a mercy from your Lord.
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters together
Added ة to make it mean one specific instance of mercy
Added tanween (ً) to show this word is receiving the action in the sentence
The doubled م shows it's connected to the previous word in pronunciation
Added fatha and doubled the middle letter to create a noun meaning 'one who does the action intensively'
Added kasra to show this word is being possessed by something
Added كَ at the end to show this means 'your Lord'
Added shadda on first letter for extra emphasis
Basic negation particle that means 'not'
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence. This word connects to تَكُونَنَّ (be) to say 'so do not be'
Connected the root letters and added basic vowels to make it pronounceable
Added تَ at the beginning to show 'you' are doing the action in present tense

steps.m: you will definitely be (a helper for the disbelievers)
steps.n: Added نَّ at the end to add strong emphasis, like saying 'definitely' or 'surely'. This word connects to ظَهِيرًا لِّلْكٰفِرِينَ (helper for the disbelievers) in the ayah.
Added vowels and ي to make a word for someone who helps or supports others
Added ًا to show this describes what someone might become. This connects to تَكُونَنَّ (become) in the ayah, warning against becoming a helper for the disbelievers.

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
This pattern shows who does the action, like changing 'write' to 'writer'
The ين ending makes it plural (more than one)

steps.m: for those who reject/cover up truth
steps.n: The لِ shows this is for or to someone/something
The ل got stronger (shadda), the ا got longer, and the word got a final َ because it connects to ظَهِيرًا (helper) in the verse
This is the basic negation word that stops or prevents something from happening

steps.m: and not (connected to يَصُدُّنَّكَ - let them not prevent you)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this negative command to what came before it. This connects to the verb يَصُدُّنَّكَ, making the complete meaning 'and let them not prevent you'
Added fatha marks and doubled middle letter to make basic past tense verb
The ي at the start shows this is happening now or in the future
The نَّ at the end makes the meaning stronger and more certain

steps.m: he surely blocks/prevents you (from عَنْ ءَايٰتِ ٱللَّهِ - from Allah's signs)
steps.n: The ك at the end shows you are being prevented. This connects to the next words 'from Allah's signs' to complete the meaning.
This is a preposition that shows something moving away from something else. In this ayah, it connects to the verb يَصُدُّنَّكَ (prevent/turn away) to show what someone is being turned away from.

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be evident
steps.n: The root relates to things that are clear signs or proofs
Forms the singular noun meaning 'sign' - something that shows or proves something else
Makes the word plural to show multiple signs. The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this form.
The kasra at the end shows this word is connected to Allah (اللَّهِ) that follows it, meaning these are Allah's signs specifically
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to The One True God.
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and shadda on ل. The word is possessed by إِذْ (when) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to create the basic noun form
Added fatha mark at end to show this word is being used as a preposition

steps.m: when (referring to أُنزِلَتْ - they were sent down)
steps.n: This word points to a specific time in the past when something happened. Here it refers to when the verses were sent down.
Basic past tense form of the verb
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he caused to descend/he sent down
steps.n: Adding أ at the start makes it about causing the action to happen
Changed vowels to show that something received the action rather than did it
Added ت to make it feminine, since it refers to a feminine word (like سورة/chapter). Links to إِلَيْكَ (to you) in the verse.
This is the basic preposition showing direction or movement towards something
The ى changes to ي before adding a suffix. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
Added ك at the end to show this is directed 'to you' specifically

steps.m: to call out to someone or invite them
steps.n: Changed to past tense plural form meaning 'they called/were called'
Added sukun on first letter and damma on second letter to make it a command. The و drops because it's a weak letter at the end of a command.
Added helping alif at start since Arabic words can't begin with a sukun
Added و with fatha at start to mean 'and'. The alif gets a wavy top (ٱ) when it comes after و.
Basic form of the word showing direction or movement toward something
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end which is just a spelling variation of regular alif. In this ayah, it points toward 'your Lord' (رَبِّكَ)
The root relates to having authority and taking care of others
Adding shadda makes the meaning more intense and complete, showing ultimate authority and care
Changed the vowel to kasra to show this word will be owned by something/someone
Adding كَ shows this is your Lord. This word follows إِلَىٰ (to) in the phrase 'call to your Lord'
This is a negative particle that means 'not' or 'do not'
Added و at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it
Added fatha on lam to help with pronunciation. This word connects to تَكُونَنَّ (you be) to say 'and do not be'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense form
Added ت at start for 'you' and changed vowels for present tense. The و stays because it's part of the root.
Added نَّ at the end to add strong emphasis, like saying 'definitely/must'. This is used in the Quran to show a strong command or prohibition.
This is a basic preposition showing source or origin
Added fatha at the end because it connects to the next word (المشركين). This helps with pronunciation when connecting words.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together

steps.m: one who joins partners with Allah
steps.n: The مُ at the start changes it from 'to join' into 'someone who joins' - like changing 'eat' to 'eater'

steps.m: those who join partners with Allah
steps.n: The ين ending makes it plural masculine

steps.m: the polytheists (those who join partners with Allah)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it definite - specific polytheists
This is the basic negation particle that tells us not to do something. Here it's telling us not to call upon something.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it

steps.m: to call upon, invite, pray to

steps.n: The root letters get vowels (fathas) and the last letter و changes to ا to make 'he called'
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, sukun on middle letter, and و at end
Changed from 'he' to 'you (feminine)' by replacing يَ with تَ

steps.m: you (feminine) call/invite (in negative context with لَا)
steps.n: Final و drops when used with لَا (don't) to make it negative - like saying 'do not call'
This is a preposition that shows accompaniment. In this ayah, it connects the word اللَّهِ (Allah) to the verb تَدْعُ (call upon/invoke), showing who is being called upon together with Allah.
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ل to show this is the unique name of The One True God
Changed to show 'Allah' is connected to another word (مَعَ/with) by adding a small i-sound (kasra) at the end

steps.m: to worship or be devoted to

steps.n: Changes from basic root into noun meaning 'a god/deity'
The small alif (ٰ) shows the 'aa' sound should be stretched when reading

steps.m: a god/deity (object of تَدْعُ - do not call upon)
steps.n: The ًا at the end shows this is the object being acted upon. In this ayah, Allah is telling us not to call upon any deity besides Him.

steps.m: to come later or be behind

steps.n: The long alif at start makes this into a word meaning 'other'
Added fatha at the end because this word follows إِلٰهًا which ends in ًا (accusative case)
This is the basic form of the negation particle used to say 'no' or 'not'

steps.m: there is not (strong negation of إِلٰهَ)
steps.n: The elongated alif (آ) makes the negation stronger. Here it strongly negates إِلٰهَ (deity) to emphasize there is absolutely no deity except Allah.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'something that is worshipped'
This word is affected by لَا (no/there is not) in the phrase لَا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ meaning 'There is no god except Him.' The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this form.
This is a primary particle used for making exceptions. It starts with hamza with kasra (إِ) followed by lām (ل)
Adding shadda (ّ) on the lām emphasizes the exception

steps.m: except (in this ayah: everything will perish except His Face)
steps.n: Adding alif (ا) at the end completes the particle. In this ayah, it introduces the exception to the statement that everything will perish - only Allah's Face will remain.
This is a personal pronoun that refers back to Allah in this ayah. It's connected to the word إِلٰهَ (deity/god) to complete the meaning 'He is the only deity'.
Adding damma vowels and shadda creates a word meaning 'every' or 'all'. This word describes شَىْءٍ (thing) in this ayah.
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to make it a noun. The hamza at the end is part of the basic word structure.
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this is indefinite (a thing) and is being acted upon by the word كُلُّ (every) that comes before it in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changes from a verb (perished) to a noun meaning 'one who perishes'

steps.m: except (excluding هُوَ from the statement لَآ إِلٰهَ)
steps.n: This is a common exception particle that doesn't come from a root. In this ayah, it's used to exclude هُوَ (He/Him) from the negative statement لَآ إِلٰهَ (there is no god), creating the meaning 'there is no god except Him'.
Root letters combined to make the simple noun 'face' - like turning something into a thing you can see

steps.m: His Face (referring to Allah's Face, being excluded from destruction)
steps.n: Added هُ (his) at the end to show it means 'His face'. The َ (fatha) shows this is being acted upon.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation

steps.m: for him/to him (connected to الْحُكْمُ - the judgment)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هُ (him) to show who the judgment belongs to. This word connects to الْحُكْمُ (the judgment) in this ayah, showing that the judgment belongs to Him (Allah).
The noun pattern shows this is the result of judging - specifically referring to authority or the power to judge

steps.m: the judgment/authority (belongs to لَهُ - to Him)
steps.n: Added the definite article ال and the nominative case ending ُ. This word is connected to لَهُ (to Him) in the ayah, showing that authority belongs to Allah.
This is the basic preposition meaning 'to' or 'towards'
The ى changes to ي before adding pronouns
Added هِ at the end to show 'him'
Added وَ at the beginning to mean 'and'. This word connects to تُرْجَعُونَ (you will be returned) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he returned' to 'you are returned' by adding تُ at start and changing vowels to show someone is being returned (passive)
Added ون to show this applies to a group of people. The word relates to إِلَيْهِ (to Him) in the ayah.
